programme.title: NCIS

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The agents are called to the scene of a car accident in which a petty officer was killed. programme.title: NCIS

programme.title: Medium
programme.desc: Paranormal drama inspired by the life of medium Allison DuBois. Patricia Arquette stars as a woman plagued by visions of the dead, who uses her unique gift to solve murder cases. In the first episode, her husband Joe gives descriptions of her dreams to law enforcement agencies across the country - and one of the images bears a resemblance to a homicide in Texas. However, she comes into conflict with a cynical ranger who is sceptical about her ability

Guest starring Elizabeth Berkley, with David Caruso and Emily Procter

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Kaye Wragg joins the cast as PC Diane Noble, who makes a good start with the Sun Hill team when she and Smithy arrest a key figure in the crack house raid. Gina is less than impressed with Heaton's heavy-handed tactics, which leave an elderly witness fighting for her life. Sam suffers a miscarriage and realises from Stuart's reaction that he isn't ready for a long-term commitment

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Borough Commander Adam Okaro drops in and discovers all is not well in Sun Hill, with Gina and John at loggerheads over the zero-tolerance policy and the bad press it is generating. Kezia sets up a sting to get a confession from Beth Adamson's stalker Keith Durante, but the case takes a sinister turn when the frightened woman disappears under the nose of her plain-clothes police protector

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. Stalker Keith Durante is finally brought in for questioning after Kezia leans on his wife to retract the alibi she gave him. However, Jack Meadows finds the interrogation a frustrating affair and resorts to devious methods to extract a confession. A fire in a B&B alerts the Sun Hill watch to an illegal immigration operation on their patch, and Sam finally accepts she has no future with Stuart

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Will returns to duty after recovering from injuries sustained in the explosion and immediately becomes a hero when he foils a suicide bid by long-suffering Louisa Durante. His moment of glory is tinged with disappointment on learning Emma is engaged to Matt, and he later discovers Dan is using steroids to boost his boxing prospects. Aware of the pressure on veterans Tony and Reg, Smithy gives them what seems to be a simple dog-napping case, but it's not long before Sun Hill has a murder inquiry on its hands

programme.title: Bramwell
programme.desc: A brothel fire causes dark secrets to be revealed and the relationships between Eleanor, Joe and Major Quarrie to be finally resolved

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's The Glass Virgin
programme.desc: Feature-length drama from the pen of the popular storyteller. A woman runs away from her wealthy family after she discovers a shocking secret about her past. Nigel Havers, Emily Mortimer and Brendan Coyle star

programme.title: dinnerladies
programme.desc: The crew prepares for the millennium dinner at the Old Brewery and it is a race against time to get things organised. Tony makes a discovery on the fire escape that puts a damper on the festivities

programme.title: dinnerladies
programme.desc: Petula's bizarre lifestyle finally catches up with her, forcing Bren to make a difficult decision, and a new member of staff commits a terrible faux pas when she makes the tea. Comedy, starring Victoria Wood, Thelma Barlow and Julie Walters

programme.title: dinnerladies diaries
programme.desc: Behind the scenes of how the British sitcom was made, featuring unseen footage of Victoria Wood, and interviews with the cast as they reminisce about the show

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: A middle-aged widow's family become convinced a photographer is only marrying her for money and ask Hetty to investigate. Given a week to probe his past and find out whether he's a gold-digging cad or a 24-carat gentleman, the unlikely sleuth unearths some extremely interesting facts. Drama based on the novel by David Cook, starring Patricia Routledge and Derek Benfield

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Barbara helps a pregnant mother-of-two whose husband has recently died, and is on hand to give her support when a further tragedy drives her and her children from their home. Lucille teaches a health and relationships class at the youth club, only to encounter a furious mother who objects to her daughter attending. Sister Monica Joan goes into hospital for cataract surgery

programme.title: Rebus
programme.desc: The detective oversteps the mark and finds himself sent back to the Police Training College to learn some professional etiquette and save his pension. Sidekick DS Siobhan Clarke is left to investigate the murder of an art dealer, but it is not long before her boss turns up on the trail of the killer. Ken Stott stars in the crime drama, with Claire Price and Jennifer Black

programme.title: Trial & Retribution
programme.desc: Lynda La Plante's crime drama. DI Pat North investigates the case of a woman abducted in broad daylight, but a sudden murder confession leaves the squad with more questions than answers. Meanwhile, DCS Michael Walker begins to fear for his children's safety at the hands of his ex-wife's new boyfriend. Kate Buffery and David Hayman star

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Nikki struggles to come to terms with life back home and seeks out fellow pathologist Sally Vaughan for support, only to learn she is nowhere to be found. National Crime Agency investigator Guy Bernhardt asks Nikki to help him by employing pathologist David Cannon, the chief suspect in Sally's disappearance. As Jack, Clarissa and Thomas share concerns about Nikki's wellbeing, a body found in a reservoir is linked to a murder case from 16 years previously. Return of the crime drama, starring Emilia Fox, with Julian Rhind-Tutt and Alex Macqueen

programme.title: Fresh Fields
programme.desc: Emma is forced to rearrange her plans for the festive period at the last minute, leaving Hester and William disappointed they will not get to spend their first Christmas with their grandson. However, it remains to be seen who will help them consume all the extra food and drink they have to hand. Christmas special of the comedy, starring Anton Rodgers and Julia McKenzie

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Peggy causes trouble for Della while Sharon and Michelle's night out hits a sour note. Ricky finds himself having housing problems, and Bianca goes up in the world

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Binnie and Della make travel plans and Ian is made an offer he can't refuse. Roy tries to say it with flowers and Michelle decides to go into property

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Hospital-grade morphine appears on the streets of Sun Hill and is soon traced back to St Hugh's, where the doctor in charge of the internal investigation proves less than cooperative with the police. Under pressure from Will, Dan admits his debt to illegal steroid use and agrees to throw his big fight, but gets carried away with the atmosphere and knocks his opponent out. Tony pays his last respects as his father passes away peacefully

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Linden is suspended after the death of a patient in his care, but defies his superiors and returns to Holby. Sam is given the all-clear after his final cancer scan, and Connie and Ric become the front-runners for the new director of surgery position

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: A power cut in the hospital leads to pandemonium, with Bex evacuating as many patients as possible in a typically tactless manner, while Harry is forced to operate in the dark. Jack is released by the police and Merlin prepares to meet his birth mother, but he is in for a life-changing shock when her identity is finally revealed

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Phil and Sam are sent to Romania in search of a missing woman thought to be the victim of a ruthless people-trafficking operation. Going undercover as a sex tourist looking for 'fresh meat', Phil finds his continental colleagues resentful of the intrusion and when his cover is blown the English duo find the streets of Bucharest are no place to be out late

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Peggy causes trouble for Della while Sharon and Michelle's night out hits a sour note. Ricky finds himself having housing problems, and Bianca goes up in the world

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Binnie and Della make travel plans and Ian is made an offer he can't refuse. Roy tries to say it with flowers and Michelle decides to go into property

programme.title: Doctor Finlay
programme.desc: Dr Napier becomes concerned at the generally poor state of women's health in Tannochbrae, and launches a bold new campaign to set matters right. Rhona falls head over heels in love, and two surprising would-be instructors step forward to teach the locals ballroom dancing. Highland medical drama, starring David Rintoul, Ian Bannen and Annette Crosbie

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: Part one of two. Tragedy looms as Andrew is swept away while trying to save an injured man from a fast-flowing river, giving Sam the opportunity to become the hero of the hour. Meanwhile, Joanna faces up to the traumatic prospect of breaking devastating news to a teenage girl. Gary Mavers and Joseph Millson star

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James's luck takes a sudden turn for the better in his romance with Helen, but there is no respite for the unfortunate Dalby family. Tristan's capacity for causing chaos shows no sign of coming to an end

programme.title: Fresh Fields
programme.desc: Hester looks to the future when a fortune teller promises money, travel and a secret admirer _ but will her dreams really come true? Comedy, starring Anton Rodgers and Julia McKenzie

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Tom and Diana set out to help the local community by taking on a scheming estate agent who has got her claws into the hapless Harvey. Graham Crowden and Stephanie Cole star in the retirement home comedy

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: Michelle tries to steal the Fallen Madonna from Herr Flick to boost the Resistance funds, while Edith, Yvette and Mimi are photographed for a 'Spirit of Nouvion' competition

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Howard is thrown into a panic when Pearl wants to know why his underpants are missing. Peter Sallis stars

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: Rosemary and Laura are employed to restore a country churchyard in time for a medieval fair, but it is not long before a local landowner is killed by an arrow beneath an ancient yew in the centre of the graveyard. The duo investigate the possibility that rival bidders for the victim's highly desirable farm are to blame - though affairs of the heart might offer a more likely motive. Drama, starring Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: DI Mooney and the team become embroiled in the mysterious world of faith healing when a blind woman dies while being treated by renowned healer Steadman King. When it turns out the victim was poisoned, all eyes turn to Steadman and his cup of holy water. But how could the prime suspect have carried out the deed in front of an audience without anyone noticing? Meanwhile, JP is put through his paces when Pico the police dog joins the team on a week-long trial, and Florence tries to recreate some of Jack's home comforts

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The death of a teenage tennis star occupies the team when it is suggested her suicide jump two years earlier was anything but. They soon find several people with motives for her murder, including her coach Nick and agent Anthony, but it seems the one with the most to benefit from the girl's demise was her great rival Fawn Brammall - a shy and subdued player who proves difficult to question. Meanwhile, Gerry wonders if he did enough to encourage his own daughter's sporting ambitions and Brian hatches a plan to turn his dog into a movie star. Detective drama, guest starring Tamzin Outhwaite and Alexei Sayle

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: Danny accuses Zoe of letting her relationship with Will interfere with her work as they pursue a rogue scientist who has created a lethal chemical weapon and plans to sell it to terrorists. He soon faces a dilemma of his own, however, when the objective of their mission changes. Keeley Hawes and David Oyelowo star

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: A woman is murdered and the forensic evidence takes the team to a cosmetic clinic where the victim had been receiving various treatments seemingly for free, but Nikki and Silva are frustrated by the CEO of Hamilton Ashe hospital when he blocks access to Karen's medical records. Meanwhile, when his daughter Rosie turns up at the Lyell as her mum is in hospital over a pregnancy complication, Thomas must play the doting father for a few weeks - a role that does not come naturally

programme.title: Jane Eyre
programme.desc: Four-part adaptation of Charlotte Bronte's novel about an orphan girl who struggles to survive the harsh treatment of a boarding school to embark on a career as a governess. Jane takes up her first position at the home of the alluring and unpredictable Edward Rochester, under the watchful eye of laundrywoman Grace Poole. Ruth Wilson, Toby Stephens and Francesca Annis star

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Jack tries to persuade a reluctant Tony to accept Eddie's offer of a kidney and Simon is shocked when Jane leaves hospital early, especially as she refuses to let him care for her. Duffy faces a life-changing decision following Ryan's return to Holby and Anna is devastated to learn the truth about Merlin. Meanwhile, Les Dennis makes a guest appearance as a bullying boss who drives an employee to suicide and finds his life in danger from her enraged husband

programme.desc: Pat and Janine prepare to go on a cruise with Roy. Binnie and Della prepare to travel. Alan's male pride is dented and Peggy's plans for the Vic face a setback

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Ricky isn't happy about Pat going away. Nigel is upset when Debbie works late. Sharon offers Peggy the licence

programme.title: Doctor Finlay
programme.desc: The Highland GP has a run-in with an old friend and later investigates what appears to be an attack on a gay man, only to find himself caught up in a traumatic domestic violence case. David Rintoul, Ian Bannen and Annette Crosbie star

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: Part two of two. Dawn rushes to be by Andrew's side as he hovers near death, while Sam is tormented by the thought that he could have done more to help his stricken colleague. Joanna wrestles with a medical dilemma as her teenage patient recoils from the prospect of undergoing the risky operation to remove her brain tumour. Starring Gary Mavers, Joseph Millson and Haydn Gwynne

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: The Dalbys' long spell of misfortune comes to an end as James stumbles on the cause of the disease afflicting the family's herd. Mrs Hall advises James to get on with it vis-a-vis Helen and warns him that he risks losing her, with Richard Edmundson still vying for her affections. James finally pops the question, but shudders at the thought of having to ask Mr Alderson's permission

programme.title: Fresh Fields
programme.desc: Hester is frustrated by William's snoring, and a neighbour's pet adds to her problems. Comedy, guest starring Stephanie Cole, with Anton Rodgers and Julia McKenzie

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: The ladies bowling team nickname Tom 'Superstud' when Diana ends up in his bed after the residents are bequeathed £200 to get drunk at a wake. Comedy, starring Graham Crowden and Stephanie Cole

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: Rene decides to run for the Spanish border but is stopped before he can make it across - and General Von Klinkerhoffen gets hold of the painting. The British airmen become trapped in wine barrels in the cellar of the chateau

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: The ladies of the village feel the need for a little excitement and hire a minibus to take them out for a picnic. However, against all the odds, their trip turns into a mystery tour. Julie T Wallace and Trevor Bannister guest star

programme.title: Miss Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. An unpopular colonel is found dead in the vicarage of St Mary Mead and investigations reveal no shortage of suspects - including the vicar and members of the victim's family. Christmas edition from 1986, starring Joan Hickson, Paul Eddington and James Hazeldine

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Sandra and the team unearth the 10-year-old case of a poet from Belfast whose burnt body was found in the scrapyard of a known gangster. Gerry becomes convinced the victim's links with the criminal fraternity led to his demise, but Brian has a different theory, believing the secret of his murder lies in his verse. Amanda Redman, Dennis Waterman and Alun Armstrong star

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: Zoe is put on trial for conspiracy to murder following an abortive mission in which an undercover police officer was killed by a gang of Turkish gun-runners. Espionage drama, guest starring Ian McNeice alongside Keeley Hawes and David Oyelowo

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: American diplomat Ryan Reed is shot dead and the team arrives at the scene to find his body posed - sitting upright on a bench with his hand tucked inside his jacket. An argument quickly ensues between the police and the US embassy, which claims the murder falls under its jurisdiction, while the Lyell crew fights against the bureaucracy of the FBI, aided only by the US deputy chief of mission Matt Garcia. However, when he goes missing, Nikki is concerned he might be the next target

programme.title: Jane Eyre
programme.desc: Jane begins to wonder about the chemistry between herself and Rochester, who disappoints her when he leaves without warning. However, he returns with a houseful of distinguished guests, including the beautiful Blanche Ingram and her opinionated mother. Things turn sour when a mysterious visitor arrives. Ruth Wilson, Toby Stephens and Francesca Annis star

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Chris Mason and Rachel Burden present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: With Jonathan Ashworth MP, Laura Kuenssberg, Fraser Nelson and Imelda Staunton. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: London Marathon
programme.desc: Gabby Logan introduces coverage of the elite men's race, with the field including world record holder and defending champion Eliud Kipchoge. British four-time Olympic champion Sir Mo Farah is the pacemaker for this year's men's race, which will take place in a bio-secure closed course that will consist of 1.5 mile laps, taking in the iconic sights of The Mall, Horse Guards Parade and Buckingham Palace. With commentary by Steve Cram and Andrew Cotter

programme.title: Politics London
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate from the region

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes. Presented by Tina Daheley

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley shares her journey of faith, from becoming a Christian as a teen to her ordination and beyond, as she visits the people, places and music that have shaped her

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson searches for a Cambridgeshire home for a young couple with a £570,000 budget who want to escape suburban Kent. Keen to explore more of the country's wildlife, Jules also visits the Fens and finds out about the UK's first nature reserves

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore salvages three items from a recycling centre in Witley, with Leigh-Anne Treadwell and Neil Wragg helping transform a sofa, some deckchairs and two tables

programme.title: Planet Earth II
programme.desc: Rare footage of the elusive animals that live in the unforgiving environment of the world's mountain ranges. A female snow leopard with a cub ends up caught between two rival males, grizzly bears rub themselves up against trees in an attempt to shed their winter fur, and golden eagles hunt over snow-capped peaks

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Ellie Harrison visit Devon, once the cider capital of England, to discover how orchards are being revived and find out why a rare bat is so at home in this region. Tom Heap investigates whether efforts to combat air pollution could come at a high cost to those rural communities already fighting fuel poverty, and Adam Henson is on a quest to ensure the survival of one of Britain's rarest cattle breeds - the Albion

programme.title: Little Mix The Search
programme.desc: The band search for a group who not only sing, but also play instruments, with the musicians auditioned individually and then put through their paces alongside other hopefuls

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness return with the motoring magazine, beginning by heading to Paddy's home town of Bolton to test three new company cars by spending 24 hours in them - with no getting out. Plus, Chris heads to Italy to put the Ferrari SF90 supercar through its paces

programme.title: Us
programme.desc: Douglas pounds the streets in a desperate search for his son, while back in the UK Connie scours the internet for clues to Albie's whereabouts and finds one on Kat's social media account. Returning to the hotel, a defeated Douglas confides in fellow lone traveller Freja about his and Connie's past, but with still no sign of Albie, and after an unexpected brush with the law, he reaches rock bottom

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, including Manchester United v Tottenham Hotspur at Old Trafford and Arsenal v Sheffield United at the Emirates Stadium. Champions Liverpool were also in action as they visited Aston Villa, while newly promoted Fulham were also on their travels, away to Wolverhampton Wanderers. Plus, a round-up of all the goals and talking points from yesterday's fixtures, and a look at the morning's headlines on the back pages

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League including Manchester City v Tottenham Hotspur and Birmingham City v Chelsea

programme.title: Squad Goals
programme.desc: West Ham suffer an 8-0 defeat to Chelsea - the heaviest in the club's history - and captain Gilly Flaherty reveals her mental health struggles as a professional footballer

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Angela Rippon, Gloria Hunniford and Julia Somerville investigate a fresh crop of scams aimed at parting people from their cash, beginning with hackers intercepting customer accounts with some of the biggest names in the fast food business, leaving the clients to foot the bill. Plus, the devious distraction tactics criminals are using to dupe unsuspecting car owners out of hundreds of pounds, and how companies are failing victims of identity fraud.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martell Maxwell follow the progress as couples and a father-and-son team tackle renovation projects in Stoke, Liverpool and Birmingham

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: A village becomes the scene of a violent gunfight, firearms officers fear for their lives when confronted by an armed man, and two paramedics are victims of a terrifying attack

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: Documentary following Scottish Ambulance Service crews in Glasgow and Edinburgh as they respond to 999 calls, including one about a child who has fallen in a loch

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Richard Madley and Stephanie Connell help two teams look for items at the Brackley Antiques Cellar in Northamptonshire, while presenter Eric Knowles views one of the largest shoe collections in the world

programme.title: The Indian Doctor
programme.desc: A smallpox epidemic in India prompts Prem's mother-in-law to visit her daughter in Trefelin, Wales, and the husband of whom she does not approve. Meanwhile, local evangelist minister Herbert Todd struggles to keep his teenage daughter Verity under control, and receptionist Gina tries to get her baby baptised. Comedy drama, starring Sanjeev Bhaskar

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers continue their culinary journey through time by celebrating Britain's numerous food festivals. The duo reveal the fascinating stories behind the events and sample some of the dishes on offer

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman heads to Shropshire and Herefordshire to help a recently retired couple with a £550,000 budget find a dream country home that is full of traditional period charm. Nicki also visits the Weston Park Model Air Show

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers come up with alternative designs for the garden of a retired couple in Worcestershire who want to get rid of their unsafe grey slab patio

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team restore three more treasured family heirlooms, working on a writing bureau, a pair of dancing clogs, and two gold-plated ploughing trophies

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas host the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Thirty-four people died in Australia last year as the worst bushfires in living memory swept across the country , with an area roughly the size of England devastated and thousands of homes destroyed. A year on, Clive Myrie hears from the people living in the path of the fires and the firefighters who risked their lives to save them, and asks if these levels of destruction are to become normal

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Mick's suspicions of Frankie increase when he finds out she is looking after Ollie and he pushes her for answers. Gray is struggling to look after his children and work on Whitney's case - and when it turns out that the first hearing is the same day as Chantelle's funeral, his colleague insists on taking him off the case. Suki finds Jags' jeans covered in blood, and Callum promises Ben he'll be at Lexi's dance recital - only for Thompson to call him into the station yet again

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Alison and Mike hit a new obstacle to nailing down the next event booking when the Captain's past threatens to be unearthed, and he wildly protests at the couple's endeavours. Meanwhile, Thomas and Julian battle it out over how the ghosts will spend their evening, Lady Button is confused by urges she hasn't felt before, and an old friend of Kitty's reappears

programme.title: Me, My Brother and Our Balls
programme.desc: Documentary in which Love Island star Chris Hughes and his brother Ben explore male fertility, looking at why the subject is not taken seriously enough. They meet one of the UK's leading experts for a step-by-step guide to sperm, hear how infertility affects masculinity from a GB Olympic rower, and consider how their fertility struggles could impact on their family, and their girlfriends, in the long term

programme.title: Stand Up for Live Comedy
programme.desc: Paddy Raff hosts the showcase for emerging comic talent from Belfast. Rachel Fairburn unpicks the joys of sisterhood, Mary O'Connell explains why she is a bad feminist and Shane Todd breaks down sectarianism over a coffee. Paddy rounds off the show with an insight into what makes a Belfast audience unique

programme.title: Sunny D
programme.desc: Comedy, written by and starring Dane Baptiste as a frustrated 29-year-old, still living at home with his parents. Dane dreads a party to celebrate his dad's 65th birthday, knowing his relatives are going to have awkward questions about his career and love life. When he tries to boost his confidence with the aid of a bottle of rum, it leads to some awkward revelations and an accidental proposal

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Actor Damian Lewis hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with journalist Katy Balls and comedian Chris McCausland joining captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host returns with a stellar line-up of guests in a show that sees him joined by esteemed actor Rupert Everett, comedian and actress Lolly Adefope and stand-up comedian and writer Sara Pascoe in the studio, and music legend Dolly Parton and Emmy-winning British star Riz Ahmed remotely. Plus, Roisin Murphy performs her single Murphy's Law and members of a reduced-size audience try to recount an anecdote from the dreaded red chair

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Dan Walker and Louise Minchin

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Angela Rippon goes on the trail of fly-tippers, getting to the heart of a problem that is infuriating communities up and down the country

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Dion Dublin follow the development progress of properties in Canterbury in Kent, London's Crystal Palace and the Cumbria village of Branthwaite

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: Two life-changing assaults cost a police officer his career and have a huge impact on his health as he is diagnosed with complex PTSD

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: A crew are called to a festival worker who needs treatment for a reaction to a bee sting, while an elderly patient with labyrinthitis needs to be taken to hospital

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning is joined by John Cameron and Gary Pe in Malvern, Worcestershire, and a regular Bargain Hunt expert talks about his love of Whitefriars Glass

programme.title: The Indian Doctor
programme.desc: Prem tries to locate Dan so he can treat him, but his efforts to keep an outbreak of smallpox in Trefelin under control suffer a major setback when the Rev Todd sparks a rebellion among his fellow villagers by refusing to be vaccinated. Meanwhile, Sgt Dawkins composes a love letter to Megan - who still has no idea how he feels about her

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers indulge in an afternoon snack as they examine the history of high tea in Britain. The pair sample a selection of tasty food and hear some fascinating stories as they embark on a quest to explore what has become a national institution

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton guides a pair of budding entrepreneurs who want to escape to the Cotswolds and launches himself onto the UK's only bobsleigh and skeleton push-start track in Bath

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich head to Weston-super-Mare, Somerset, where they face an oddly shaped backyard with an assortment of random levels and surfaces

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team restore three more treasured family heirlooms, working on a Victorian railway lamp, a musical jewellery box and a Brownie toadstool

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat. Presented by Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Thompson tells Callum they are not getting enough from the listening device and suggests that he should start wearing a wire - much to his horror. Whitney tells Gray he should be with his children for the funeral even though she is terrified of facing court without him. Secrets fester between Mick and Linda, and Ash is horrified by what Suki has done

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming
programme.desc: Enforcement officers visit a London bar with unpaid debts concerning work on the air conditioning, and a classic car dealer - but the man they meet at the car lot claims to have just taken over the premises. Plus, the case of a cleaner who took her employer to an employment tribunal over unpaid sick and holiday pay

programme.title: Life
programme.desc: Three weeks on, the residents each face decisions about their future. Gail tries to remain dedicated to her marriage, while Belle finds raising Maya has an impact on her love life. David meets Kelly's university friends, and is left uncertain how well he knows her, while a chance encounter sees him reunited with Saira. Andy has a tense altercation with Liam that forces him to consider what he really wants out of life

programme.title: Just One Night
programme.desc: Simi and Prescillia test their commitment before deciding whether to move in together

programme.title: Just One Night
programme.desc: Phoebe and Ruairi test whether they are strong enough to maintain a relationship between two countries as Phoebe looks to study abroad

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness return with the motoring magazine, beginning by heading to Paddy's home town of Bolton to test three new company cars by spending 24 hours in them - with no getting out. Plus, Chris heads to Italy to put the Ferrari SF90 supercar through its paces

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: Filmed in the autumn of 2019, cameras follow a new cast of characters from the London Ambulance Service as they care for the nine million people of the city. In the third episode, crew members in the capital respond to reports of an armed male and two stabbings. Elsewhere, Adam and Orani are dispatched to a long-term heroin user whose concerned mum has called for help

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: At Longmeadow, Monty Don revels in the early autumn colour in his garden, reviews how the tomatoes he grew from a late sowing have got on this season and gives advice on planting onions for next year. Rachel de Thame reveals how the new garden project she started in the spring has developed over the summer and Fish, of the rock band Marillion, gives a tour of his garden and reveals how it has helped his mental health as well as the creative process of making music. Plus, in Germany, Arit Anderson finds out about the transformation of a disused industrial site into a thriving and beautiful park

programme.title: London Marathon
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents coverage of the 40th London Marathon, live from The Mall, where the women's elite race is the first to get under way. The event takes place in very different circumstances this year, as the leading racers in the world will battle it out for the titles, while around 45,000 runners will take part in the ‘virtual' London Marathon. With commentary by Steve Cram and Andrew Cotter

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Sally Phillips take a look at the week's talking points and explore the ethical and religious issues of the day

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights from throughout the series, offering viewers a chance to revisit some of their favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: Mary Berry's Simple Comforts
programme.desc: Mary explores how families use food to create their own sense of home, including a father and daughter who have taken their home cooking to the streets with their own food van. Later in the programme, Mary makes some of her own family favourites, including a hearty bolognaise bake with a burrata and heritage tomato salad, and a paneer and roast vegetable curry

programme.title: London Marathon
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents continued live coverage of the 40th London Marathon, featuring the men's and women's wheelchair races. This year's event involves elite athletes only due to the Coronavirus pandemic, but the day will see 45,000 runners from around the world run the 26.2 mile distance as part of the ‘virtual' London Marathon - and some of their own inspirational stories will be featured throughout the broadcast. With commentary by Andrew Cotter and Tanni Grey-Thompson

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A look at the TV appearances made over the years by the Oscar-winning actor John Wayne, capturing the milestones and highlights of his life and career that led to him becoming considered a US national icon. Narrated by Sylvia Syms

programme.title: The Searchers (1956)
programme.desc: An embittered American Civil War veteran sets out to find his niece, who has been abducted by a renegade Comanche tribe. His search takes him many years, and during the long quest his travelling companion starts to wonder if he intends to rescue her or kill her. John Ford's Western, starring John Wayne, Natalie Wood, Jeffrey Hunter and Vera Miles

programme.title: Golf: Scottish Open
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents highlights of the final day's play from the Renaissance Club on the north Berwickshire coast, where Bernd Wiesberger triumphed last year, beating Benjamin Hebert in a play-off. Overnight leader Wiesberger finished two-under for the day and 22-under overall, with France's Hebert carding a stunning nine-under round of 62. However, it was the Austrian who triumphed in dramatic style on the third sudden-death hole after a three-putt bogey by Hebert

programme.title: Britain's Biggest Dig
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts and Dr Yasmin Khan reveal how Birmingham's Victorian working class residents made their home city one of the most important industrial centres on the planet. They examine the surviving remains of one of the world's oldest railway terminuses, and discover how many of the thousands of skeletons in the cemetery have evidence of bone damage or amputation, as well as signs of the after-effects of industrial pollution. Plus, a look at the true story behind Peaky Blinders

programme.title: Michael Palin: Travels of a Lifetime
programme.desc: Michael Palin revisits his travel series, beginning with Around the World in 80 Days. He draws on his personal archive of audio recordings and diary notebooks, and reflects on how he became a seasoned global traveller. The first episode revisits his visits to Venice, Cairo, Saudi Arabia, India, Hong Kong and China - a nation that was closed to most westerners at the time. Featuring contributions from fans and friends including David Attenborough, Joanna Lumley and Simon Reeve

programme.title: 25 Siblings & Me
programme.desc: The incredible story of Oli, a young man with Asperger's syndrome, who overnight discovered he has 25 siblings from the same American sperm donor dad. Desperate to be part of this new family, Oli travels to the USA, meeting his donor and joining a mass sibling reunion

programme.title: Better Things
programme.desc: Wider family and friends start gathering at the Fox household to celebrate Frankie's 15th birthday with a 'Batceanera', a mixed cultural event shared with her friend Rosita

programme.title: Better Things
programme.desc: Sam takes Duke and Murray to an LA Dodgers game, but has difficulty finding a ride home. Xander arrives for a family meal but gets a taste of his own medicine

programme.title: London Marathon
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents action from the 40th London Marathon, featuring the elite men's, women's and wheelchair races. Commentary by Steve Cram, Andrew Cotter and Tanni Grey-Thompson

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Sylar's murderous rampage continues and leads to a confrontation with Peter, while Nathan makes important decisions based on Linderman's shocking revelations. Thompson uses every means possible to hunt for Claire, and Hiro's determination to save the world grows stronger in the face of adversity. Guest starring Eric Roberts (The Dark Knight)

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: The future holds disappointment for Hiro when he discovers that people with secret powers are considered terrorists. As the time-traveller prepares for a showdown with the President he confronts some familiar faces who have changed over the years

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the political debate, inviting a panel of guests from the worlds of politics, media and showbusiness to answer questions on subjects that have made the headlines over the past seven days

programme.title: Sue Perkins: Along the US-Mexico Border
programme.desc: Part two of two. Sue visits the city of Juarez, which adjoins El Paso on the other side of the border, the two effectively forming a single large city spreading across two nations. A 57-mile section of border wall runs through the two cities, but does not stop thousands of people coming and going across every day. Sue confronts her fear of horses to try out a Mexican form of dressage and meets a woman living in the US who has been separated from her husband, who is not legally allowed to enter the US

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison and Joe Crowley are in the Lake District to celebrate the 250th anniversary of William Wordsworth's birth, and also mark 50 years of Langdale and Ambleside Mountain Rescue. Plus, Adam Henson find out where he will get the best prices for his rams - online or at a traditional livestock market?

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Carole Baxter creates a bog garden from scratch in her rural garden and George Anderson is in his allotment, where he checks on his apples and pears. Brian Cunningham puts his no-mow garden to bed for the winter and adds some sparkles of spring interest and Kirsty Wilson is in her flat looking at reviving interest in a plant that has gone out of fashion, the African violet

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul and Bob fish for brown trout on the river Test in Hampshire, using a Victorian fly-casting technique pioneered in the area as well as an authentic 19th-century rod. The Mayflies are hatching, and the famously short-lived insects inspire some thoughts on the ephemeral nature of life, and what they would do if they only had 24 hours left

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament, presented by David Cornock.

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Decimate
programme.desc: A team of three must keep the £20,000 cash wall intact by answering questions correctly. However, if they are Decimated, they will leave with nothing. Quiz show, hosted by Shane Richie

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: Paul Martin is in Nottingham, where vintage fan Melissa Mason and jewellery designer Paolo Moscardini learn the tricks of the buying trade to see who can make the most profit

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: When Woody and Smiler find yet another thing wrong with the Turners' house they have to evacuate to the motel, where George encounters Dean - a feared nemesis from his childhood

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The sewing room is transported back to the 1960s as the six remaining contestants revive vintage garments, take on time-honoured techniques and wrestle with antique equipment in an attempt to show Patrick Grant and Esme Young they deserve a place in the quarter finals. For their first task they are asked to follow a pattern for a colour block dress, then must work with PVC to transform a rain mac in the alteration challenge, before creating fitted jackets inspired by the era. Claudia Winkleman presents

programme.title: Gorilla Family and Me
programme.desc: Part one of two. Wildlife cameraman Gordon Buchanan travels to the Democratic Republic of the Congo, where he realises a lifelong dream by spending time with Grauer's gorillas, the largest primates on earth. He meets the fearsome alpha male Chimanuka and two babies, one a lonely orphan and as Gordon gains the trust of these mysterious, seldom seen animals, he discovers their incredible compassion and gets caught up in a battle between giant rival silverbacks for control of the family

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Mark Stacey and Michael Baggott assess items brought into Newby Hall in North Yorkshire. Mark comes across an interesting Clarice Cliff cake plate and Michael enjoys an ancient Chinese urn. Presenter Paul Martin takes in the splendours of the country house's Adams interiors

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Former winners Rick Edwards, Scarlett Moffatt, Naga Munchetty and David O'Doherty test their general knowledge skills on this week's special House of Games: Champion editions

programme.title: Fish Town
programme.desc: James Cosmo narrates a documentary about the port of Peterhead and the people working on land and at sea to put fish on the nation's plates. The fishing boat Reliance, skippered by John Clarke, heads to the Shetland Isles in search of whitefish and prawns, while the Ocean Endeavour ventures to Rockall, one of the UK's most remote fishing grounds, in search of haddock

programme.title: The Yorkshire Dales
programme.desc: Adventurer Paul Rose's trip concludes in Wharfedale, where he talks to some of the women whose story inspired the film Calendar Girls, finds out why bats are under threat in the area and meets families from inner-city Bradford who are having their first Dales experience

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: The return of the quiz in which contestants answer questions on specialist subjects and then test their general knowledge

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts another first-round match as shuffleboard players the Sliders take on live entertainment fans the Ticket Collectors. The teams must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between four things that may appear at first not to be linked, with one set of clues consisting of Hoopoe, Kittiwake, Chiffchaff, and Cuckoo

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The penultimate first-round match of the academic quiz sees four students from the University of Edinburgh pitting their wits against a team from the University of Manchester in a bid to reach the second stage of the competition. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions

programme.title: In the Face of Terror
programme.desc: Documentary following the stories of ordinary people fighting for truth and justice following acts of terror. The first one featured is a hostage crisis in Syria that culminated in seven beheadings by a British executioner, later dubbed Jihadi John, while the second looks at the far-right attack in Christchurch, New Zealand, in which 51 people were shot dead in the city's mosques

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Angela Barnes, Ed Byrne, Ed Gamble, Milton Jones and Nish Kumar join host Dara O Briain and regular Hugh Dennis for the topical comedy show, looking back at the week's events through the medium of fast-thinking and improvisation

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef takes a culinary break in Bordeaux, where he helps judge a local wine contest and hires a 2CV for an excursion to the coast - abundant with mussels and oysters

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Stacey Dooley Investigates
programme.desc: The presenter visits a women's prison in America that has adopted a radical new approach to incarceration based on giving the inmates a sense of purpose and self-esteem. She meets eight prisoners who are serving life sentences for murder, and discovers the complex sexual relationships that have developed among many of the inmates

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison and Joe Crowley are in the Lake District to celebrate the 250th anniversary of William Wordsworth's birth, and also mark 50 years of Langdale and Ambleside Mountain Rescue. Plus, Adam Henson find out where he will get the best prices for his rams - online or at a traditional livestock market?

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: Staff of London Ambulance Service gear themselves up for the fall out from another day of intense politics in the city, as hundreds of thousands of protesters take to the streets. Despite all their planning in how to handle the crowds, the team in control are taken by surprise by a problem no one had foreseen - the entire computer system used to respond to 999 calls crashes. Elsewhere, staff receive a call about a patient who is not breathing, and Advanced Paramedic Kev is immediately dispatched to the cardiac arrest

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: An unusual piece of sporting history arrives for the attention of silversmith Brenton West and leather expert Suzie Fletcher - a decorative belt awarded to a Victorian speed walker. A 1950s metal mobo roundabout and a dented trumpet are also restored. Presented by Jay Blades

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: A rural village becomes the scene of a violent gunfight and a firearms team fear for their lives after being confronted by an armed man

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Adam Frost celebrates some of the glorious gardens the programme has visited over the last few years and gives plenty of seasonal hints and tips for viewers to be doing in our own gardens. Frances Tophill visits a lochside retreat, Joe Swift finds lots of take home tips from a city garden, Carol Klein is inspired by plant combinations in West Yorkshire and Advolly Richmond shows how a historical landscape was designed for fun and frolic. Plus, the couple who let nature be their gardening guide and a gardener who makes containers her focus of attention

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Decimate
programme.desc: Three people who have never met before must work as a team and answer questions correctly in a bid to win a share of a £20,000 cash prize. Quiz show, hosted by Shane Richie

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: In Exeter, former air traffic controller Paul Johnson takes on behavioural safety consultant Keith Jackson in the antiques challenge. Along the way presenter Paul Martin teaches them the tricks of the trade and the dos and don'ts of how to be an antique dealer

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: A statue of Weld's celebrated founder has been desecrated and Constable Tom challenges George to go one better and solve the mystery of who did it

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The quarter-final sees the five remaining contestants tackle three activewear challenges, beginning by following a pattern to make a man's Lycra cycling top, which involves a complex zip insertion. Next, they transform garish 1980s ski suits into children's jackets, before they end by creating yoga outfits - the most fitted garments ever attempted on the programme. Judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young then decide which one leaves the competition and misses out on the semi-final. Claudia Winkleman presents

programme.title: Gorilla Family and Me
programme.desc: Part two of two. Gordon Buchanan returns to the Democratic Republic of Congo in the hope of finding the gorilla family again, and discovering the outcome of silverback Chimanuka's confrontation with a rival. He soon finds the apes, but discovers their lives are now under threat from poachers, and works with the local rangers to prevent them coming to harm

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Compilation edition revisiting some of the historic venues from the series and offering some new valuations and unexpected auctions. Paul Martin visits Rodmarton Manor, an extraordinary Arts and Crafts house in the Cotswolds

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Rick Edwards, Scarlett Moffatt, Naga Munchetty and David O'Doherty test their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games, with a winner declared on Friday

programme.title: Fish Town
programme.desc: The trawler Reliance is halfway through a fishing trip off the Shetland Isles when skipper John Clarke spots a broken rope that could lead to the boat losing its net. The crew fix the problem and put out the nets again, but they are hit by a rogue wave that rips a two-ton metal bar off the top of the boat. In the remote Norwegian sector of the north sea, the trawler Rosebloom is out hunting for hake when a Norwegian coastguard boat announces that officers are coming aboard to inspect it

programme.title: Blitz: The Bombs That Changed Britain
programme.desc: The story of a tight-knit community of ship builders and factory workers in Clydebank, West Dunbartonshire, and how their lives were changed by the bombings of March 1941. The documentary focuses on the after-effects of one particular explosive, which destroyed number 78 Jellicoe Street, killing 15 members of the Rocks family. Surviving relatives and friends recall how they were deeply affected by the events, and how the return of the bombers led to a mass exodus from the town

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: In the Irish resort of Skerries, the crew run to the station when strong winds sweep a teenager on a paddleboard towards the busy shipping lanes of the Irish Sea, while in the Bristol Channel, among the many yachts taking advantage of a fresh summer breeze, one loses a crew member overboard. While the crowds are heading home at the end of a busy summer's day in Cleethorpes, two 13-year-old girls find themselves alone, cut off and at risk of drowning on a sandbank rapidly disappearing under a fast-rising tide

programme.title: Surgeons: At the Edge of Life
programme.desc: David Jenkins, a world-leading surgeon at Royal Papworth, performs one of the most demanding operations possible, draining a patient's body of blood to remove clots in her lungs. At Addenbrooke's hospital, consultant orthopaedic surgeon Andrew Carrothers is also pushing at the boundaries of medical science with a complex operation that he has devised called the Harrington Plus. Frustrated by the lack of options for patients whose pelvis and hip bones have been badly weakened by cancer, Andrew added a crucial stage to an existing operation to enable cancer patients to walk again

programme.title: The Ranganation
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan hosts an isolation version of his topical comedy show, in which a diverse group of 20 members of the public drawn from all walks of life are invited to share their thoughts on the state of modern Britain today

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: The World's War: Forgotten Soldiers of Empire
programme.desc: Part one of two. Historian and film-maker David Olusoga tells the story of First World War from the perspective of those of who made it a truly global conflict - the millions of African and Asian troops and ancillaries who served alongside Europeans. He begins by examining how the British and the French drew immediately on the military resources of their respective empires at the outbreak of the conflict, with Sikhs, Gurkhas, Garhwalis and Pathans from the Raj and Spahis, Zouaves and Senegalese from north and west Africa being among the first to experience the horrors of trench warfare

programme.title: The World's War: Forgotten Soldiers of Empire
programme.desc: Part two of two. Historian and film-maker David Olusoga explores how Germany enlisted Muslims from north Africa and the Middle East to join the fight against Britain and its allies during the First World War. Muslim PoWs and deserters held in special camps in Germany were indoctrinated to see Kaiser Wilhelm II as a lover of Islam and a supporter of national independence wherever the British or French flags flew. General Paul von Lettow-Vorbeck led an army of east African troops in an insurgency that cost the lives of hundreds of thousands of black troops

programme.title: Best of Top Gear
programme.desc: Highlights of the past two series of the motoring show, including the time the team took a Yorkshire road trip in the latest sports cars from Ferrari, Aston Martin and Porsche. Plus, Paddy McGuinness building his own luxurious off-roader from scratch and Chris Harris putting the Rolls-Royce Cullinan through its paces

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a selection of eight of the most extraordinary sequences from Planet Earth II and Blue Planet II, with a new soundtrack by Hans Zimmer, Jacob Shea and the team at Bleeding Fingers - performed by the BBC Concert Orchestra and Mercury Award-winning rapper Dave on the grand piano. The film ranges from the East Cape of South Africa to the north of Norway and from Chile to the Indian Ocean to show how animals overcome adversity to survive and thrive in some of the world's most challenging environments

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: A Garden in Snowdonia
programme.desc: A year in the life of Bodnant Garden in North Wales. With visitor numbers in decline, head gardener Troy Scott Smith and his manager Michael McLaren are spending £2million on a revamp, with the aim of creating one of the top 10 gardens in the world

programme.title: Vienna Summer Night Concert 2020
programme.desc: The Vienna Philharmonic's annual Summer Night Concert takes place in Vienna's Schonbrunn Palace Gardens. Postponed from May, the 2020 show is conducted by Russian maestro Valery Gergiev and features German tenor Jonas Kaufmann. Adopting the theme of love, it features arias by Jules Massenet, Emmerich Kalman and Giacomo Puccini

programme.title: Around the World in 80 Days
programme.desc: Michael Palin sets off from London en route to Italy for the first leg of his attempt to emulate the legendary adventures of Phileas Fogg. After an overnight stay in Venice, the intrepid explorer helps the local refuse collectors before boarding the Espresso Egitto ferry bound for Alexandria

programme.title: Around the World in 80 Days
programme.desc: The ferry Michael Palin hopes to catch in Suez to take him across the Red Sea develops engine trouble and the next ship out is the following day. Missing his connection in Jeddah, he is granted permission to drive across the desert of Saudi Arabia - without his film crew - in an attempt to get his journey back on schedule

programme.title: The Hector: From Scotland to Nova Scotia
programme.desc: Neil Oliver recounts the story of the 1773 highland migrants who left Scotland to settle in Nova Scotia. He uncovers their terrifying journey in a filthy disease-ridden ship, the Hector, and goes on to meet some of their descendants

programme.title: Munro: Mountain Man
programme.desc: The story of Victorian adventurer Hugh Munro and his determination to list and climb all the Scottish mountains whose height exceeds 3,000ft. Nicholas Crane visits some of the country's spectacular mountains as he explores the adventurer's bid to reach more than 500 peaks and reveals how his name has been adopted by climbing enthusiasts pursuing the same goal, a pastime known as Munro bagging

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Whitney is hiding something and Bianca is determined to get to the bottom of it. Jase struggles to get a job, and Roxy must choose between Sean and Ronnie

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Bianca realises where Whitney's money has come from, Dawn is feeling the strain of poverty until Jase turns up with a solution, and Billy pitches a business plan to Ian

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: A killer spider is on the loose, Dawn is torn over whether to accept Jase's mone, and Ronnie's attempts to move on with her life are short-lived when Max plants a seed of revenge

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Dawn is in the money and determined to rub it in her neighbours' face, and Mo's attempt to capitalise on Charlie's spider bite has terrible consequences for Billy

programme.title: Earth's Great Rivers
programme.desc: Following the astonishing wildlife, landscapes and people of the world's longest river, the Nile, beginning in the tropical mountain range of the Rwenzoris, home to one of the world's strangest birds, the shoebill stork. Then, cameras follow the Nile's journey as it travels through the wild Savannah lands of Uganda, before reaching the Ethiopian highlands where it receives an injection of water far to the East

programme.title: A Garden in Snowdonia
programme.desc: Garden supervisor Adam Salvin restores an impressive Italianate terrace to its former glory at Bodnant Garden in north Wales, while Dave Larter shares his passion for giant lilies. The show also examines the world-famous laburnum arch in full bloom

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints an autumn scene depicting a landscape with a mountain in the distance, snuggled in bright green trees and bushes and mirrored in glistening waters

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and art expert Philip Mould visit a church in Lancashire, once patronised by Yorkshire's famous Bronte sisters, that is the home of a painting thought to have been produced by an Italian old master. The picture itself is a pieta, an image of the aftermath of Christ's crucifixion, and Philip has a hunch it may be from the Italian Renaissance, possibly making this one of the oldest pieces featured in the series so far. On behalf of the church's congregation, Fiona and Philip travel to Italy to compare the Lancastrian pieta with works by old masters Titian and Tintortetto, while back in the UK, Bendor Grosvenor investigates the history of a local aristocrat who is believed to have donated the painting to the church more than 200 years ago

programme.title: The Secret History of Writing
programme.desc: Lydia Wilson looks at the increasing use of the Latin alphabet, which in some languages means more than changing letters as it entails a change of identity.

programme.title: The Brits Who Designed the Modern World
programme.desc: Arts reporter Brenda Emmanus marks the opening of Kensington's new Design Museum this month by profiling 10 of the most celebrated living British designers, whose work has revolutionised the nation's homes, streets and culture since the Second World War. Among those whose careers are highlighted are road-sign designer Margaret Calvert, ZX Spectrum creator Rick Dickinson, Andrew Ritchie, who gave the world the 'Brompton Bike', and Kenneth Grange, who designed everything from British Rail's distinctive InterCity 125 to Kenwood food mixers. A panel of contributors including Will.i.am, Jeremy Paxman, Pete Waterman and Ade Adepitan explain how their lives have benefited from British designs

programme.title: Pedalling Dreams: The Raleigh Story
programme.desc: Documentary telling the tale of the ups and downs of Raleigh bikes, using personal testimony and rare and previously unseen archive film. Born in the back streets of Nottingham in 1888, Raleigh grew to become the biggest bicycle manufacturer in the world and, for more than a century, was known for its simple and practical bikes

programme.title: The Joy of Painting

programme.title: Earth's Great Rivers
programme.desc: A journey along the American river, whose fingers stretch into nearly half of the USA, collecting water from 31 states. The documentary follows the Mississippi through varied landscapes from its source to its mouth, starting in the towering Rocky Mountains of Wyoming and Montana, where billions of tons of water, ultimately destined to flow south are trapped as ice and snow

programme.title: Australia with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The adventurer meets members of one of the most notorious and feared motorcycle gangs in Gold Coast, before heading to the suburbs to meet a vet and his team rescuing and treating injured koalas. In Sydney, he chats to a billionaire who made his fortune in housing for new migrants from Asia and visits the country's first Muslim women's Australian rules football team. On the last leg of his journey, Simon takes to the air to witness devastating and deadly fires ripping through the Bush, before celebrating Australia Day at his final destination of Melbourne

programme.title: Inside Cinema
programme.desc: The first of four profiles marking Black History Month. Ellen E Jones narrates an exploration of the Get Out writer and director's career as a comedian, film-maker and producer in movies and TV, looking at how a lifelong love for the horror genre has informed his style

programme.title: Inside Cinema

programme.title: TV's Black Renaissance: Reggie Yates in Hollywood
programme.desc: Documentary exploring how a wave of hit shows written by and starring African-American talent is shaking up the TV industry. programme.desc: Waffle's been awarded the class toy for good behaviour, and Zoe and Callum have big news.

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: JoJo & Gran Gran go to the Library and JoJo gets her first ever library card!

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: Scarf Lady helps Sarah and Duck catch the fluffs and puffs to refill their pancaked pillows

programme.title: Moon and Me
programme.desc: Lambkin must catch the tub wen it rolls away with Nana inside it

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: The Soup Dragon cheers Tiny up when the Iron Chicken goes away on a trip to see the Wols

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: Makka Pakka's Og-pog runs away and only the Pontipine children know how to stop it

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Floella Benjamin reads Baby Badger's Wonderful Night, by Karen Saunders and Dubravka Kolanovic

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
