programme.title: London Marathon
programme.desc: Gabby Logan introduces coverage of the elite men's race, with the field including world record holder and defending champion Eliud Kipchoge. British four-time Olympic champion Sir Mo Farah is the pacemaker for this year's men's race, which will take place in a bio-secure closed course that will consist of 1.5 mile laps, taking in the iconic sights of The Mall, Horse Guards Parade and Buckingham Palace. With commentary by Steve Cram and Andrew Cotter

programme.title: Politics Wales
programme.desc: All the latest political news and debate from Wales and beyond, presented by James Williams

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes. Presented by Tina Daheley

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley shares her journey of faith, from becoming a Christian as a teen to her ordination and beyond, as she visits the people, places and music that have shaped her

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson searches for a Cambridgeshire home for a young couple with a £570,000 budget who want to escape suburban Kent. Keen to explore more of the country's wildlife, Jules also visits the Fens and finds out about the UK's first nature reserves

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore saves three things about to be dumped at a tip, transforms them into bespoke and valuable pieces, before selling them and giving the profits to the people who were getting rid of them in the first place

programme.title: A Special School
programme.desc: Nurse Leanne responds to a medical emergency when a pupil has a seizure and is having difficulty breathing, while hairdresser Claire volunteers her time in the school's salon to work with pupils who find it extremely difficult and sometimes painful to have their hair

programme.title: Hidden Wales with Will Millard
programme.desc: The writer and adventurer explores the hidden pieces of history from the Second World War. Wales made a huge contribution to the war effort and the remote countryside was witness to many military activities. Remnants from that time can still be found, but they lie hidden. Will seeks some of them out by visiting plane crash sites on the Brecon Beacons, uncovering an old training academy on the Great Orme, gaining exclusive access to an MoD training base that played a vital role in the conflict and tracks down the secret bunkers of a home-based army of saboteurs

programme.title: Coal House
programme.desc: Having exchanged modern conveniences for the harsh realities of life in a mining community of the 1920s, the mothers find themselves working 14-hour days while the men toil at the coal face and the children tackle a new curriculum at school. Narrated by Nicola Reynolds

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Ellie Harrison visit Devon, once the cider capital of England, to discover how orchards are being revived and find out why a rare bat is so at home in this region. Tom Heap investigates whether efforts to combat air pollution could come at a high cost to those rural communities already fighting fuel poverty, and Adam Henson is on a quest to ensure the survival of one of Britain's rarest cattle breeds - the Albion

programme.title: Little Mix The Search
programme.desc: The band search for a group who not only sing, but also play instruments, with the musicians auditioned individually and then put through their paces alongside other hopefuls

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness return with the motoring magazine, beginning by heading to Paddy's home town of Bolton to test three new company cars by spending 24 hours in them - with no getting out. Plus, Chris heads to Italy to put the Ferrari SF90 supercar through its paces

programme.title: Us
programme.desc: Douglas pounds the streets in a desperate search for his son, while back in the UK Connie scours the internet for clues to Albie's whereabouts and finds one on Kat's social media account. Returning to the hotel, a defeated Douglas confides in fellow lone traveller Freja about his and Connie's past, but with still no sign of Albie, and after an unexpected brush with the law, he reaches rock bottom

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, including Manchester United v Tottenham Hotspur at Old Trafford and Arsenal v Sheffield United at the Emirates Stadium. Champions Liverpool were also in action as they visited Aston Villa, while newly promoted Fulham were also on their travels, away to Wolverhampton Wanderers. Plus, a round-up of all the goals and talking points from yesterday's fixtures, and a look at the morning's headlines on the back pages

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League including Manchester City v Tottenham Hotspur and Birmingham City v Chelsea

programme.title: Squad Goals
programme.desc: West Ham suffer an 8-0 defeat to Chelsea - the heaviest in the club's history - and captain Gilly Flaherty reveals her mental health struggles as a professional footballer

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Angela Rippon, Gloria Hunniford and Julia Somerville investigate a fresh crop of scams aimed at parting people from their cash, beginning with hackers intercepting customer accounts with some of the biggest names in the fast food business, leaving the clients to foot the bill. Plus, the devious distraction tactics criminals are using to dupe unsuspecting car owners out of hundreds of pounds, and how companies are failing victims of identity fraud.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martell Maxwell follow the progress as couples and a father-and-son team tackle renovation projects in Stoke, Liverpool and Birmingham

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: A village becomes the scene of a violent gunfight, firearms officers fear for their lives when confronted by an armed man, and two paramedics are victims of a terrifying attack

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: Documentary following Scottish Ambulance Service crews in Glasgow and Edinburgh as they respond to 999 calls, including one about a child who has fallen in a loch

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Richard Madley and Stephanie Connell help two teams look for items at the Brackley Antiques Cellar in Northamptonshire, while presenter Eric Knowles views one of the largest shoe collections in the world

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: The Indian Doctor
programme.desc: A smallpox epidemic in India prompts Prem's mother-in-law to visit her daughter in Trefelin, Wales, and the husband of whom she does not approve. Meanwhile, local evangelist minister Herbert Todd struggles to keep his teenage daughter Verity under control, and receptionist Gina tries to get her baby baptised. Comedy drama, starring Sanjeev Bhaskar

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers continue their culinary journey through time by celebrating Britain's numerous food festivals. The duo reveal the fascinating stories behind the events and sample some of the dishes on offer

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman heads to Shropshire and Herefordshire to help a recently retired couple with a £550,000 budget find a dream country home that is full of traditional period charm. Nicki also visits the Weston Park Model Air Show

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers come up with alternative designs for the garden of a retired couple in Worcestershire who want to get rid of their unsafe grey slab patio

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team restore three more treasured family heirlooms, working on a writing bureau, a pair of dancing clogs, and two gold-plated ploughing trophies

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas host the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: X-Ray
programme.desc: The consumer rights show returns, with Steffan Powell looking at the cost of online gaming and the team getting money back for families whose holidays were cancelled during lockdown. Rachel Treadaway-Williams investigates a shocking data breach by one of the country's biggest electrical firms

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Mick's suspicions of Frankie increase when he finds out she is looking after Ollie and he pushes her for answers. Gray is struggling to look after his children and work on Whitney's case - and when it turns out that the first hearing is the same day as Chantelle's funeral, his colleague insists on taking him off the case. Suki finds Jags' jeans covered in blood, and Callum promises Ben he'll be at Lexi's dance recital - only for Thompson to call him into the station yet again

programme.title: Wales' Black Miners
programme.desc: Documentary telling the extraordinary story of Wales' black miners, the unsung heroes of the coal industry who have been left out of the history books. Former footballer Nathan Blake goes on a mission to unearth the truth about these brave men who toiled shoulder-to-shoulder with white miners, finding out who they were, and what kind of lives they led above and below ground

programme.title: Me, My Brother and Our Balls
programme.desc: Documentary in which Love Island star Chris Hughes and his brother Ben explore male fertility, looking at why the subject is not taken seriously enough. They meet one of the UK's leading experts for a step-by-step guide to sperm, hear how infertility affects masculinity from a GB Olympic rower, and consider how their fertility struggles could impact on their family, and their girlfriends, in the long term

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Thirty-four people died in Australia last year as the worst bushfires in living memory swept across the country , with an area roughly the size of England devastated and thousands of homes destroyed. A year on, Clive Myrie hears from the people living in the path of the fires and the firefighters who risked their lives to save them, and asks if these levels of destruction are to become normal

programme.title: Stand Up for Live Comedy
programme.desc: Paddy Raff hosts the showcase for emerging comic talent from Belfast. Rachel Fairburn unpicks the joys of sisterhood, Mary O'Connell explains why she is a bad feminist and Shane Todd breaks down sectarianism over a coffee. Paddy rounds off the show with an insight into what makes a Belfast audience unique

programme.title: Sunny D
programme.desc: Comedy, written by and starring Dane Baptiste as a frustrated 29-year-old, still living at home with his parents. Dane dreads a party to celebrate his dad's 65th birthday, knowing his relatives are going to have awkward questions about his career and love life. When he tries to boost his confidence with the aid of a bottle of rum, it leads to some awkward revelations and an accidental proposal

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Actor Damian Lewis hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with journalist Katy Balls and comedian Chris McCausland joining captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host returns with a stellar line-up of guests in a show that sees him joined by esteemed actor Rupert Everett, comedian and actress Lolly Adefope and stand-up comedian and writer Sara Pascoe in the studio, and music legend Dolly Parton and Emmy-winning British star Riz Ahmed remotely. Plus, Roisin Murphy performs her single Murphy's Law and members of a reduced-size audience try to recount an anecdote from the dreaded red chair

programme.desc: Prem tries to locate Dan so he can treat him, but his efforts to keep an outbreak of smallpox in Trefelin under control suffer a major setback when the Rev Todd sparks a rebellion among his fellow villagers by refusing to be vaccinated. Meanwhile, Sgt Dawkins composes a love letter to Megan - who still has no idea how he feels about her

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers indulge in an afternoon snack as they examine the history of high tea in Britain. The pair sample a selection of tasty food and hear some fascinating stories as they embark on a quest to explore what has become a national institution

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton guides a pair of budding entrepreneurs who want to escape to the Cotswolds and launches himself onto the UK's only bobsleigh and skeleton push-start track in Bath

programme.title: Garden Rescue

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team restore three more treasured family heirlooms, working on a Victorian railway lamp, a musical jewellery box and a Brownie toadstool

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat. Presented by Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Thompson tells Callum they are not getting enough from the listening device and suggests that he should start wearing a wire - much to his horror. Whitney tells Gray he should be with his children for the funeral even though she is terrified of facing court without him. Secrets fester between Mick and Linda, and Ash is horrified by what Suki has done

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming
programme.desc: Enforcement officers visit a London bar with unpaid debts concerning work on the air conditioning, and a classic car dealer - but the man they meet at the car lot claims to have just taken over the premises. Plus, the case of a cleaner who took her employer to an employment tribunal over unpaid sick and holiday pay

programme.title: Life
programme.desc: Three weeks on, the residents each face decisions about their future. Gail tries to remain dedicated to her marriage, while Belle finds raising Maya has an impact on her love life. Andy has a tense altercation with Liam that forces him to consider what he really wants out of life

programme.title: Just One Night
programme.desc: Simi and Prescillia test their commitment before deciding whether to move in together

programme.title: River City
programme.desc: The drama returns with new epsiodes for the first time since filming was halted due to the coronavirus pandemic. Scarlett is comforted as Shieldinch bids a fond farewell to an old friend, Poppy comes to a worrying conclusion about the unwanted attention she is receiving, while Caitlin tries to ignore health concerns. Plus, a dangerous shadow looms large over an unsuspecting Lenny

programme.title: Black & Scottish
programme.desc: Stewart Kyasimire talks to inspirational black people across Scotland about their lives, including Sex Education star Ncuti Gatwa, professor Sir Geoff Palmer, and Scotland's first Rasta councillor Graham Campbell. Stewart gives them the opportunity to pass on their experience to the next generation - in the shape of his eight-year-old daughter Yasmin

programme.title: Life
programme.desc: Three weeks on, the residents each face decisions about their future. Gail tries to remain dedicated to her marriage, while Belle finds raising Maya has an impact on her love life. David meets Kelly's university friends, and is left uncertain how well he knows her, while a chance encounter sees him reunited with Saira. Andy has a tense altercation with Liam that forces him to consider what he really wants out of life

programme.title: Just One Night

programme.title: Top Gear

Plus, Chris heads to Italy to put the Ferrari SF90 supercar through its paces

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: Filmed in the autumn of 2019, cameras follow a new cast of characters from the London Ambulance Service as they care for the nine million people of the city. In the third episode, crew members in the capital respond to reports of an armed male and two stabbings. Elsewhere, Adam and Orani are dispatched to a long-term heroin user whose concerned mum has called for help

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Chris Mason and Rachel Burden present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Leeds United v Manchester City and Chelsea v Crystal Palace. Leeds have already been involved in some high-scoring matches on their return to the top flight, and they were set to face another test of their credentials against a City side looking to regain the title

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: With Jonathan Ashworth MP, Laura Kuenssberg, Fraser Nelson and Imelda Staunton. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: London Marathon
programme.desc: Gabby Logan introduces coverage of the elite men's race, with the field including world record holder and defending champion Eliud Kipchoge. British four-time Olympic champion Sir Mo Farah is the pacemaker for this year's men's race, which will take place in a bio-secure closed course that will consist of 1.5 mile laps, taking in the iconic sights of The Mall, Horse Guards Parade and Buckingham Palace. With commentary by Steve Cram and Andrew Cotter

programme.title: Sunday Politics Northern Ireland
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers looks at the political developments of the week and questions policy-makers on the key issues

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes. Presented by Tina Daheley

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley shares her journey of faith, from becoming a Christian as a teen to her ordination and beyond, as she visits the people, places and music that have shaped her

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson searches for a Cambridgeshire home for a young couple with a £570,000 budget who want to escape suburban Kent. Keen to explore more of the country's wildlife, Jules also visits the Fens and finds out about the UK's first nature reserves

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore salvages three items from a recycling centre in Witley, with Leigh-Anne Treadwell and Neil Wragg helping transform a sofa, some deckchairs and two tables

programme.title: Planet Earth II
programme.desc: Rare footage of the elusive animals that live in the unforgiving environment of the world's mountain ranges. A female snow leopard with a cub ends up caught between two rival males, grizzly bears rub themselves up against trees in an attempt to shed their winter fur, and golden eagles hunt over snow-capped peaks

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Ellie Harrison visit Devon, once the cider capital of England, to discover how orchards are being revived and find out why a rare bat is so at home in this region. Tom Heap investigates whether efforts to combat air pollution could come at a high cost to those rural communities already fighting fuel poverty, and Adam Henson is on a quest to ensure the survival of one of Britain's rarest cattle breeds - the Albion

programme.title: Little Mix The Search
programme.desc: The band search for a group who not only sing, but also play instruments, with the musicians auditioned individually and then put through their paces alongside other hopefuls

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness return with the motoring magazine, beginning by heading to Paddy's home town of Bolton to test three new company cars by spending 24 hours in them - with no getting out. Plus, Chris heads to Italy to put the Ferrari SF90 supercar through its paces

programme.title: Us
programme.desc: Douglas pounds the streets in a desperate search for his son, while back in the UK Connie scours the internet for clues to Albie's whereabouts and finds one on Kat's social media account. Returning to the hotel, a defeated Douglas confides in fellow lone traveller Freja about his and Connie's past, but with still no sign of Albie, and after an unexpected brush with the law, he reaches rock bottom

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, including Manchester United v Tottenham Hotspur at Old Trafford and Arsenal v Sheffield United at the Emirates Stadium. Champions Liverpool were also in action as they visited Aston Villa, while newly promoted Fulham were also on their travels, away to Wolverhampton Wanderers. Plus, a round-up of all the goals and talking points from yesterday's fixtures, and a look at the morning's headlines on the back pages

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League including Manchester City v Tottenham Hotspur and Birmingham City v Chelsea

programme.title: Squad Goals
programme.desc: West Ham suffer an 8-0 defeat to Chelsea - the heaviest in the club's history - and captain Gilly Flaherty reveals her mental health struggles as a professional footballer

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Angela Rippon, Gloria Hunniford and Julia Somerville investigate a fresh crop of scams aimed at parting people from their cash, beginning with hackers intercepting customer accounts with some of the biggest names in the fast food business, leaving the clients to foot the bill. Plus, the devious distraction tactics criminals are using to dupe unsuspecting car owners out of hundreds of pounds, and how companies are failing victims of identity fraud.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martell Maxwell follow the progress as couples and a father-and-son team tackle renovation projects in Stoke, Liverpool and Birmingham

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: A village becomes the scene of a violent gunfight, firearms officers fear for their lives when confronted by an armed man, and two paramedics are victims of a terrifying attack

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: Documentary following Scottish Ambulance Service crews in Glasgow and Edinburgh as they respond to 999 calls, including one about a child who has fallen in a loch

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Richard Madley and Stephanie Connell help two teams look for items at the Brackley Antiques Cellar in Northamptonshire, while presenter Eric Knowles views one of the largest shoe collections in the world

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: The Indian Doctor
programme.desc: A smallpox epidemic in India prompts Prem's mother-in-law to visit her daughter in Trefelin, Wales, and the husband of whom she does not approve. Meanwhile, local evangelist minister Herbert Todd struggles to keep his teenage daughter Verity under control, and receptionist Gina tries to get her baby baptised. Comedy drama, starring Sanjeev Bhaskar

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers continue their culinary journey through time by celebrating Britain's numerous food festivals. The duo reveal the fascinating stories behind the events and sample some of the dishes on offer

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman heads to Shropshire and Herefordshire to help a recently retired couple with a £550,000 budget find a dream country home that is full of traditional period charm. Nicki also visits the Weston Park Model Air Show

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers come up with alternative designs for the garden of a retired couple in Worcestershire who want to get rid of their unsafe grey slab patio

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team restore three more treasured family heirlooms, working on a writing bureau, a pair of dancing clogs, and two gold-plated ploughing trophies

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas host the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Life Is Magic
programme.desc: Illusionist Joel M from Bangor, Co Down, takes his conjuring skills to parties and events around the country, helping to create life-changing celebrations that people will talk about for years to come. In the first edition he meets the student formal committee at Our Lady of Lourdes High School in Ballymoney to help plan an epic trick for their formal dance

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Mick's suspicions of Frankie increase when he finds out she is looking after Ollie and he pushes her for answers. Gray is struggling to look after his children and work on Whitney's case - and when it turns out that the first hearing is the same day as Chantelle's funeral, his colleague insists on taking him off the case. Suki finds Jags' jeans covered in blood, and Callum promises Ben he'll be at Lexi's dance recital - only for Thompson to call him into the station yet again

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Thirty-four people died in Australia last year as the worst bushfires in living memory swept across the country , with an area roughly the size of England devastated and thousands of homes destroyed. A year on, Clive Myrie hears from the people living in the path of the fires and the firefighters who risked their lives to save them, and asks if these levels of destruction are to become normal

programme.title: Me, My Brother and Our Balls
programme.desc: Documentary in which Love Island star Chris Hughes and his brother Ben explore male fertility, looking at why the subject is not taken seriously enough. They meet one of the UK's leading experts for a step-by-step guide to sperm, hear how infertility affects masculinity from a GB Olympic rower, and consider how their fertility struggles could impact on their family, and their girlfriends, in the long term

programme.title: True North
programme.desc: Like much of the UK's shipbuilding industry, Harland & Wolff suffered a steady decline in recent years and in the summer of 2019 it entered administration - appearing to spell the end of the company after almost 160 years. This documentary tells the inside story of the Belfast workers who helped rescue the company from closure, with access to the workers, union reps, administrators and bosses at the heart of the struggle

programme.title: Stand Up for Live Comedy
programme.desc: Paddy Raff hosts the showcase for emerging comic talent from Belfast. Rachel Fairburn unpicks the joys of sisterhood, Mary O'Connell explains why she is a bad feminist and Shane Todd breaks down sectarianism over a coffee. Paddy rounds off the show with an insight into what makes a Belfast audience unique

programme.title: Sunny D
programme.desc: Comedy, written by and starring Dane Baptiste as a frustrated 29-year-old, still living at home with his parents. Dane dreads a party to celebrate his dad's 65th birthday, knowing his relatives are going to have awkward questions about his career and love life. When he tries to boost his confidence with the aid of a bottle of rum, it leads to some awkward revelations and an accidental proposal

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Actor Damian Lewis hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with journalist Katy Balls and comedian Chris McCausland joining captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host returns with a stellar line-up of guests in a show that sees him joined by esteemed actor Rupert Everett, comedian and actress Lolly Adefope and stand-up comedian and writer Sara Pascoe in the studio, and music legend Dolly Parton and Emmy-winning British star Riz Ahmed remotely. Plus, Roisin Murphy performs her single Murphy's Law and members of a reduced-size audience try to recount an anecdote from the dreaded red chair

programme.desc: The sense of systemic racial injustice in policing that has fuelled the Black Lives Matter movement is shared far beyond the shores of the United States. In Britain it is two decades since a top level inquiry into London's police force found it to be institutionally racist - how much has really changed? HARDtalk's Stephen Sackur speaks to Leroy Logan, one of London's top black policemen until his retirement seven years ago. How easy is it to root out discrimination dressed in a police uniform?

programme.title: Call air Cladach Ile (The Loss on Islay's Shore)
programme.desc: The story of two sea tragedies that brought the First World War to the Hebrides. In February 1918, the troopship Tuscania was torpedoed by a German U-boat. More than 200 American soldiers and British crew died as lifeboats were dashed against Islay's cliffs. Eight months later, HMS Otranto collided with another ship in its convoy in a fierce storm, killing nearly 500 men. Just 19 reached Islay's shore alive

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz wants to cure Magoo's near-sightedness for good. He and Weasel go over to his house, only to be taken for Magoo's parents on a surprise visit.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz writes a genius plan on a note that gets stuck to Magoo's forehead. When Weasel tries to get it back, Magoo thinks he's being pursued by spies.

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: Alan's driving lessons with Parker take a much more urgent turn when burglars kidnap Lady Penelope and Aunt Sylvia. Action adventure, with the voices of Rasmus Hardiker, David Graham, Rosamund Pike and Sylvia Anderson

programme.title: Dare Master
programme.desc: Can Danny handle the pressure of performing basketball tricks in front of hundreds of fans and avoid the messy forfeit?

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: When Irma's scout master is disabled, Mr Bean steps in to take care of Irma and her scouts. Chaotic cartoon capers following the further adventures of the popular halfwit immortalised in the live-action TV series and Hollywood film. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Mr Bean inadvertently becomes a teacher for a class of unruly pupils. Chaotic cartoon capers following the further adventures of the popular halfwit immortalised in the live-action TV series and Hollywood films. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Ronni Ancona, Adam Gemili, Dave Johns and Joel Dommett pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Martin & Roman's Sunday Best!
programme.desc: Actor-comedian Rufus Hound, Australian singer-songwriter Delta Goodrem and presenter Kirsty Gallacher join Martin and Roman Kemp for the Sunday morning entertainment show, featuring music, chat and a host of fun and games

programme.title: Tipping Point: Best Ever Finals
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts this compilation of the arcade-themed game show's most dramatic finales, in which the last contestant standing tries to get the golden jackpot counter from the top shelf down to the win zone

programme.title: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh
programme.desc: Alan welcomes actors Lesley Joseph and Hugh Dennis to his Hampshire farm and takes a look back to the allotment where his passion for gardening began. He also meets upholsterer Micaela Sharp and renowned garden photographer Clive Nichols. Plus, more from the organic halal farming family the Radwans

programme.title: All Around Britain
programme.desc: Ranvir Singh and Alex Beresford explore the secrets of Highclere Castle in Hampshire - which will be familiar to fans of Downton Abbey. Stories include the business of brewing cider at the Thatcher family's Somerset orchard and a visit to the north east coast to learn about the past and present of Holy Island oysters

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani present live coverage from Paris, as two of the top horses in the world, Enable and Love, go head to head in the Prix De L'Arc de Triomphe. Enable, who won this race in 2017 and 2018, has been primed for a third win after her defeat 12 months ago, while Love has taken all before her this year with wins at Newmarket, Epsom and York

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A stately home is turned into a theme park celebrating the work of local horror writer Ellis Bell, but the opening ceremony is marred by the death of a film star. Barnaby and Troy's investigation uncovers the secrets within an eccentric family, and a centuries-old village feud comes under the spotlight. Murder mystery, written by Alan Plater and guest starring Beth Goddard

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. Tim, Lorna, Hayley and Adam work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts as MasterChef judge Gregg Wallace, comedian Chris Ramsey and model and presenter Vogue Williams take part in the game show, in which they must answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping counters down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - with the highest scorer playing for the £20,000 jackpot for their chosen charity

programme.title: Family Fortunes
programme.desc: Gino D'Acampo hosts this revamped version of the popular game show, which sees two families trying to guess the answers to survey-based questions posed to 100 people. The family who score the most points go through to Big Money, which sees two of them answering just five questions for the £30,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Singapore Grip
programme.desc: While the Blacketts conspire against him, Matthew learns the true nature of life and love in Singapore. As he and Vera get closer, Walter petitions the Governor and Joan hatches a plan of her own. Meanwhile, the Japanese tighten their grip on the continent. Second World War drama, starring Luke Treadaway, David Morrissey, Elizabeth Tan and Georgia Blizzard

programme.title: The Savoy
programme.desc: Documentary offering a glimpse into the lives of those lucky enough to check into the glamorous London hotel, and those who serve them. It's the start of the new year, and after their busy festive period, the hotel has high hopes for 2020 - looking at how they can improve all areas of their service and make it the most successful year in the hotel's history. Head butler Sean is determined to make his newest recruit - 37-year-old former actor Michael - the perfect Savoy butler, and is putting him to the test. As well as having to juggle a multitude of requests, Sean wants Michael to host an exclusive cocktail party for regular VIP guests, Mr and Mrs Cuming, in their suite

programme.title: London Zoo: An Extraordinary Year
programme.desc: Part two of two. Jimmy the northern white-cheeked gibbon is missing the visitors and wondering when they will be back to see him again, while keepers Mick and Joe use the quiet time to build the confidence of rare Bactrian camel Genghis, helping him to venture outside of his paddock. The elephant keepers at Whipsnade are desperate for the Asian elephants to mate, although it seems there are no such problems with the small-clawed otters when keeper Laura notices they have had their first babies

programme.title: Motorsport UK
programme.desc: Richard John Neil presents action from Brands Hatch, including the British F4 Championship and the Mini Dunlop Challenge

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Celebrity interviews and studio discussion from a female perspective

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: A family of five from Cambridgeshire answers questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then tries to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Quiz, hosted by Warwick Davis

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Mark, Gary, Tara and Rachel pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Belle might regret being in cahoots with Andrea, and there's a moment of clear attraction between Chas and Al when they head for a picnic. Manpreet is stressed, but tries to get philosophical about her situation with Meena, and is heartened when Rishi offers her his full support

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Steve and Leanne join forces to give Oliver a fighting chance, Billy confides his worries about Paul to David, and Sally and Tim plan a secret wedding

programme.title: Inside Animal A&E
programme.desc: At the Blue Cross in Grimsby, Labrador Scooby is in to have lumps removed from around his eye, while receptionist Hannah has overcome a major hurdle in starting her vet nurse training and is helping in the kennels. Alison is in theatre to operate on a rat that has a growth in his stomach - and the vets reveal some of the more unusual animals they have been called upon to treat

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Tracy drops a bombshell on Steve, Tim orders Geoff to stay away from his wedding and Billy updates Eileen on the search for Todd

programme.title: Prince William: A Planet for Us All
programme.desc: Documentary following the Duke of Cambridge as he embarks on a campaign to champion global action on conservation and climate change. As he meets inspiring local heroes around Britain and the world, he sees an opportunity to become a bridge between the young people who are crying out for change and the leaders and policy-makers who can make it happen

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Virgin's clubhouse manager Holly showers passengers with her VIP treatment Valentine-style, while officers Terry and Dave have to deal with an unhappy upgraded passenger. programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Weasel tests out Fizz's latest invention, a three-piece swimsuit to make beachgoers look chic. Magoo mistakes him for the President.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz wants to cure Magoo's near-sightedness for good. He and Weasel go over to his house, only to be taken for Magoo's parents on a surprise visit.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz writes a genius plan on a note that gets stuck to Magoo's forehead. When Weasel tries to get it back, Magoo thinks he's being pursued by spies.

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: Alan's driving lessons with Parker take a much more urgent turn when burglars kidnap Lady Penelope and Aunt Sylvia. Action adventure, with the voices of Rasmus Hardiker, David Graham, Rosamund Pike and Sylvia Anderson

programme.title: Dare Master
programme.desc: Can Danny handle the pressure of performing basketball tricks in front of hundreds of fans and avoid the messy forfeit?

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: When Irma's scout master is disabled, Mr Bean steps in to take care of Irma and her scouts. Chaotic cartoon capers following the further adventures of the popular halfwit immortalised in the live-action TV series and Hollywood film. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Mr Bean inadvertently becomes a teacher for a class of unruly pupils. Chaotic cartoon capers following the further adventures of the popular halfwit immortalised in the live-action TV series and Hollywood films. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Ronni Ancona, Adam Gemili, Dave Johns and Joel Dommett pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Martin & Roman's Sunday Best!
programme.desc: Actor-comedian Rufus Hound, Australian singer-songwriter Delta Goodrem and presenter Kirsty Gallacher join Martin and Roman Kemp for the Sunday morning entertainment show, featuring music, chat and a host of fun and games

programme.title: Tipping Point: Best Ever Finals
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts this compilation of the arcade-themed game show's most dramatic finales, in which the last contestant standing tries to get the golden jackpot counter from the top shelf down to the win zone

programme.title: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh
programme.desc: Alan welcomes actors Lesley Joseph and Hugh Dennis to his Hampshire farm and takes a look back to the allotment where his passion for gardening began. He also meets upholsterer Micaela Sharp and renowned garden photographer Clive Nichols. Plus, more from the organic halal farming family the Radwans

programme.title: All Around Britain
programme.desc: Ranvir Singh and Alex Beresford explore the secrets of Highclere Castle in Hampshire - which will be familiar to fans of Downton Abbey. Stories include the business of brewing cider at the Thatcher family's Somerset orchard and a visit to the north east coast to learn about the past and present of Holy Island oysters

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani present live coverage from Paris, as two of the top horses in the world, Enable and Love, go head to head in the Prix De L'Arc de Triomphe. Enable, who won this race in 2017 and 2018, has been primed for a third win after her defeat 12 months ago, while Love has taken all before her this year with wins at Newmarket, Epsom and York

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A stately home is turned into a theme park celebrating the work of local horror writer Ellis Bell, but the opening ceremony is marred by the death of a film star. Barnaby and Troy's investigation uncovers the secrets within an eccentric family, and a centuries-old village feud comes under the spotlight. Murder mystery, written by Alan Plater and guest starring Beth Goddard

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. Tim, Lorna, Hayley and Adam work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts as MasterChef judge Gregg Wallace, comedian Chris Ramsey and model and presenter Vogue Williams take part in the game show, in which they must answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping counters down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - with the highest scorer playing for the £20,000 jackpot for their chosen charity

programme.title: Family Fortunes
programme.desc: Gino D'Acampo hosts this revamped version of the popular game show, which sees two families trying to guess the answers to survey-based questions posed to 100 people. The family who score the most points go through to Big Money, which sees two of them answering just five questions for the £30,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Singapore Grip
programme.desc: While the Blacketts conspire against him, Matthew learns the true nature of life and love in Singapore. As he and Vera get closer, Walter petitions the Governor and Joan hatches a plan of her own. Meanwhile, the Japanese tighten their grip on the continent. Second World War drama, starring Luke Treadaway, David Morrissey, Elizabeth Tan and Georgia Blizzard

programme.title: The Savoy
programme.desc: Documentary offering a glimpse into the lives of those lucky enough to check into the glamorous London hotel, and those who serve them. It's the start of the new year, and after their busy festive period, the hotel has high hopes for 2020 - looking at how they can improve all areas of their service and make it the most successful year in the hotel's history. Head butler Sean is determined to make his newest recruit - 37-year-old former actor Michael - the perfect Savoy butler, and is putting him to the test. As well as having to juggle a multitude of requests, Sean wants Michael to host an exclusive cocktail party for regular VIP guests, Mr and Mrs Cuming, in their suite

programme.title: London Zoo: An Extraordinary Year
programme.desc: Part two of two. Jimmy the northern white-cheeked gibbon is missing the visitors and wondering when they will be back to see him again, while keepers Mick and Joe use the quiet time to build the confidence of rare Bactrian camel Genghis, helping him to venture outside of his paddock. The elephant keepers at Whipsnade are desperate for the Asian elephants to mate, although it seems there are no such problems with the small-clawed otters when keeper Laura notices they have had their first babies

programme.title: Motorsport UK
programme.desc: Richard John Neil presents action from Brands Hatch, including the British F4 Championship and the Mini Dunlop Challenge

programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness presents a special edition of the matchmaking show, celebrating its tenth series and 10 years since the first edition of its parent show, Taken Out, aired in Australia. In a twist on the usual format, Paddy gives 30 men who have previously appeared on the show a chance to take a seat 'behind the lights', while a selection of female celebrities replace the boys in the 'Love Lift'. The host also catches up with some of the couples who found lasting love after meeting on the show

programme.desc: Cleveland is disappointed to learn Donna's big sister Janet and her two unruly children are joining the family for Thanksgiving, and plots to drive her out of town. However, the plan backfires when she and Holt fall in love and elope to Las Vegas

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Lyndsey tells Alan she is moving away for a fresh start, but despite her plans to leave, the pair end up in bed together, forcing Alan juggle two women once again

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Lyndsey starts looking for a new apartment and asks Alan to move in with her, but he wonders about leaving the beach house behind. Meanwhile, Walden breaks up with Ms McMartin. Starring Jon Cryer and Courtney Thorne-Smith

programme.title: Iain Stirling's CelebAbility
programme.desc: Iain Stirling is joined by new team captain Scarlett Moffatt as she leads a group of three friends hoping to win £5,000 by defeating four celebrities in fun games based on unusual abilities the famous faces believe they possess. This time, three friends take on celebrities Lee Ryan, Helen Flanagan, Matt Evers and Nathan Caton

programme.title: Totally Bonkers Guinness World Records
programme.desc: Matt Edmondson narrates another selection of records, including the heaviest train pulled with a beard, the furthest distance as a human arrow and the smallest man's suit

programme.title: ITV2 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy and Robin spend an evening at home alone - and the absence of a chaperone allows the trainee chef's amorous side to surface. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin comes home with a puppy, to Chrissy's dismay. Flat-share comedy, with Richard O'Sullivan, Sally Thomsett and Paula Wilcox

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Long-running drama with the residents of England's most famous cobbled street

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Long-running soap following the ups and downs of village life amid the rolling scenery of the Yorkshire Dales

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: When the sleuth's dentist is found dead just minutes after he leaves his surgery, Chief Inspector Japp believes it to be suicide - but Poirot has reason to think otherwise and embarks on his own investigation. Drama, starring David Suchet

programme.title: Carry On Screaming (1966)
programme.desc: A sinister boffin, his sultry sister and two genetic misfits kidnap innocent women from spooky Hocomb Woods and turn them into mannequins - but two dim-witted detectives look set to throw a spanner in the works. Comedy horror, starring Kenneth Williams, Fenella Fielding, Harry H Corbett, Charles Hawtrey, Jim Dale, Joan Sims and Bernard Bresslaw

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A visiting American academic delivers a controversial speech, with many audience members worried his ideas could be used to target ethnic minorities. So when he is found hanged the next morning and Dr Hobson quickly discounts suicide, Lewis and Hathaway realise they have many suspects - from the local anti-racism activist who was first in voicing her disgust at the professor's beliefs to the women who had bombarded him with threatening e-mails. But then another body turns up and the case takes on a new dimension. David Soul and Patrick Baladi guest star

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby's secret past as a spy is revealed when a former agent is killed after a cricket match between the villagers and the gentry. The dead man's wounds appear to have been inflicted by a giant claw, triggering rumours of a 'beast of Midsomer', and before long, another body turns up with similar injuries. Barnaby then realises the incidents are linked to the time the local network of spies spent in Cold War Berlin. John Nettles and Jason Hughes star, with guest appearances by Anna Massey, Peter Davison and Neil Stuke

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's Hidden Caribbean: Havana to Haiti
programme.desc: Part one of two. The actress travels across two of the most enigmatic countries in the Caribbean - Cuba and Haiti - to explore, uncover and share the hidden gems they have to offer. Joanna kicks off her adventure in a Havana boxing gym with one of Cuba's rising female boxing stars. She visits the stars of the cabaret at the National Hotel that pre-dates the revolution, and entertains the cigar workers while they roll cigars in their factory. She visits the small town of Hershey, built on the success of the sugar industry, and visits the tomb of Che Guevara before heading off to Hemingway's favourite beach

programme.title: Kate & Koji
programme.desc: Despite Kate being sued after an accident in the cafe, she refuses to back down and Koji is reluctant to get involved. Although Medium is convinced she should just apologise and get it over with, Kate suspects the legal threats may have something to do with Councillor Bone. Comedy, starring Brenda Blethyn and Jimmy Akingbola

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. When the detective's old neighbour tells him she has found a gun in her teenage daughter's bedroom, his first instinct is to quietly retrieve the weapon. However, Helen persuades him to do things by the book and leads a firearms unit to take possession of it - but an innocent man is critically injured in the process, leading to an internal investigation that threatens to break up Banks's team

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. On a desperate search for his abducted daughter, Banks puts pressure on Al to lead him to Jaff, without success. However, Al later offers to help - at a price - and the detective contemplates breaking every rule in the book to meet his demands. Meanwhile, the result of Cabbot's internal investigation has led to Morton's immediate suspension. With her career seemingly in the balance, Tracy in danger and Banks's relationship with Annie close to breaking point, how far will he go to keep his family together? Crime drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is called in to investigate when the UK's prime minister and his secretary are kidnapped in France. However, he has only a day and a half to solve the case before an important conference is set to take place. Drama, starring David Suchet

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A Belgian film star arrives in London and summons her friend Poirot to unravel the mysterious threats she has been receiving, which demand the return of a priceless diamond, the Western Star. Rosalind Bennett guest stars

programme.title: Griff's Great Britain
programme.desc: Griff Rhys Jones explores the Highlands as he goes in search of Britain's second-largest bird of prey, travelling through the scenic and ancient landscape by a variety of means - but will he be able to track down the elusive golden eagle?

programme.title: Griff's Great Britain
programme.desc: Griff Rhys Jones explores the pleasures and perils of Dartmoor National Park - the largest and wildest area of open country in the south of England. He discovers it is home to ancient woodlands, high granite tors and vast tracts of rolling moorland, as well as churches, cream teas, llamas and legends

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Long-running soap following the ups and downs of village life amid the rolling scenery of the Yorkshire Dales

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Long-running drama with the residents of England's most famous cobbled street

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Kelly hints to Ashley about them getting a flat. Jack is embarrassed in hospital when Martin gives him a bed bath. Ken visits a solicitor to find out his rights over Daniel

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jack is not happy about Sarah going off to Spain. Social services come to talk with the Cairns family. Kim attempts to seduce Lord Alex

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Lord Alex tries to quietly leave Home Farm after spending the night with Kim. Jack and Rachel insist that what happened between them is over

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An accidental shooting at Ashfordly Hall prompts Bellamy to investigate reports accusing acting estate manager Richard Bentham of exploiting youngsters for child labour, but his enquiries reveal a far more complicated situation. Peggy discovers a promising new source of revenue when she realises there is a market for guided tours of the area, but her excitement is cut short by a terrifying encounter. Rural drama, guest starring former Emmerdale actor Peter Amory, with Mark Jordon, Rupert Vansittart and Gwen Taylor

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin's hopes for a night of romance are dashed when Chrissy decides to supervise his girlfriend's visit. Comedy, with Richard O'Sullivan and Paula Wilcox

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy needs a partner for the annual staff dance, so Robin takes lessons from Mrs Roper. Flat-sharing comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan, Paula Wilcox and Brian Murphy

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: George's jealousy rears its ugly head, but Mildred is in no hurry to put his mind at rest

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Dour DI Jack Frost suspects a man he arrested years before for a series of bizarre assaults on children could have returned to the area and recommenced his unsavoury activities. He tracks down the culprit, who swears he has turned over a new leaf, and decides not to charge him - a decision that shocks fellow sleuth DS Maud, whose misgivings are later justified in tragic fashion. Starring David Jason, Peter Gunn, Susannah Doyle, Bruce Alexander, John Lyons and Gwyneth Powell

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A local man confesses to a girl's murder of 11 years previously, prompting Sgt Miller to reopen the case. A search fails to uncover the victim's body, but the investigation takes an intriguing twist when PC Younger unearths a male skeleton instead. Meanwhile, Peggy sets her sights on a wealthy widower. Rural drama, guest starring Graham Turner and Geoffrey Hutchings, with John Duttine, Steven Blakeley and Gwen Taylor

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kathy and Eric argue about the incident in her flat. Linda presses Zoe to find out why Sophie has left Emmerdale. Jack tells Rachel he will give up everything for her

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jack apologises to Rachel about the previous night. Tara thanks Kim for saving her. Sophie tells Zoe she cannot bear it if she is ashamed of her

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jamie looks after the pigeons while Jack is in hospital. Emily offers Ken support in his fight to get Daniel back. Deirdre tells Barlow they need to talk

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alf celebrates getting his licence renewed. Fred fears he has ruined his chances with Rita. Vera struggles with the Rovers when half the staff call in sick

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: DCI Barnaby launches a murder investigation when the severed hand of a missing hotel manager is found by a dog. The dead man had recently been named in the will of the hotel owner, and solving the case takes on a renewed sense of urgency when fatal accidents start happening to the other beneficiaries. Mystery, guest starring Rosemary Leach and Samantha Bond, with John Nettles and Daniel Casey

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A woman's body is pulled from the river, reminding Frost that he investigated the death of her husband's first wife years ago. Unfortunately, it turns out that a key witness's statement went astray during the earlier case - which leads to Frost being suspended from duty. Discreetly continuing his enquiries, he is intrigued to learn the local priest recently heard a very disturbing confession. Starring David Jason, Eoin McCarthy, Anthony Calf, Kenneth Cope and Isla Blair

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: When a spate of petty thefts culminates in a series of murders at a student hostel, Miss Lemon persuades the Belgian sleuth to investigate. As ever, he receives support from Chief Inspector Japp. Mystery, starring David Suchet, with Philip Jackson, Pauline Moran, Damian Lewis and Paris Jefferson

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: The crime drama starring Stephen Tompkinson returns with the first of a two-part story. When the detective suffers a personal loss, he's forced to navigate a complex murder case while grieving. A young Estonian woman has been found dead, with the evidence suggesting she was buried alive. Piecing together her movements, the team uncovers a dark world of prostitution and drugs, and investigates her connection to a single father, his autistic son, and a family who seem to have silenced the town

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is called in to investigate when the UK's prime minister and his secretary are kidnapped in France. However, he has only a day and a half to solve the case before an important conference is set to take place. Drama, starring David Suchet

Discreetly continuing his enquiries, he is intrigued to learn the local priest recently heard a very disturbing confession. Starring David Jason, Eoin McCarthy, Anthony Calf, Kenneth Cope and Isla Blair

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin uses the threat of a roving mouse as an excuse to spend the night in Chrissy's bedroom. Comedy series, with Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy discovers she has an admirer, while Mr Roper thinks his wife is seeing someone else. Comedy series, with Richard O'Sullivan and Brian Murphy

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: George's brother discovers documents that prove Mildred married an Army deserter. Comedy, starring Yootha Joyce and Brian Murphy

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A woman's body is pulled from the river, reminding Frost that he investigated the death of her husband's first wife years ago. Unfortunately, it turns out that a key witness's statement went astray during the earlier case - which leads to Frost being suspended from duty. Discreetly continuing his enquiries, he is intrigued to learn the local priest recently heard a very disturbing confession. Starring David Jason, Eoin McCarthy, Anthony Calf, Kenneth Cope and Isla Blair

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A group of young offenders come to stay at Ashfordly Hall, but soon find themselves under suspicion after a spate of disturbances in the village. Meanwhile, Blaketon and Ventress go on the hunt for a spinster's missing dog, and a £50 reward gives Peggy an idea. Rural drama, starring Rupert Vansittart, William Simons, Derek Fowlds and Gwen Taylor

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Tony and Becky anxiously wait for Will's GCSE results. Alex tells Kim he feels guilty about their one-night stand. Lisa admits to Mandy she is jealous of Zak's feelings for Becky

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Lisa is still upset with Zak for flirting with Becky. Sophie tells Kim that she wants to move in with Zoe. Steve confronts Kim with some unwelcome home truths and she storms out

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alec is furious that Fiona has let him down. Curly is stunned when Angie Freeman looks him up. Liz receives a call from a frightened Steve. Vera can't cope anymore in the pub

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ken enjoys a weekend with Daniel. Fiona celebrates her 21st birthday. Alec agrees to help Vera out at the Rovers. Judy accuses Joyce of dipping into other people's catalogue money

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A horse whisperer disappears in Newton Magna, bringing Barnaby face to face with long-standing adversary Melvyn Stockard. The former convict's social climbing has paid dividends, sending him straight to the top of the village pecking order - and some believe his driving ambition makes him capable of foul play. Murder mystery, guest starring Larry Lamb (EastEnders, Gavin & Stacey), with John Nettles and Daniel Casey

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Pawnbroker Peter Lawson is shot dead in an armed robbery at his premises and Frost's investigations unearth a tragic family secret. He is convinced Lawson was killed by one of his children in revenge for years of systematic sexual abuse - but the truth turns out to be even more chilling. David Jason stars, with Diane Keen, Isla Blair, Matt Bardock, Anthony Bate, Mark Lambert and Edward Purver

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Midsomer University science fellow George Jeffers threatens to make public a problem with his state-of-the-art invention Kernel Logic, which he believes is potentially lethal, but American software boss Clinton Finn fears he will lose millions if the truth comes out. When a schoolteacher is killed in a hit-and-run, Barnaby suspects Jeffers was the intended target. Detective drama, guest starring David Haig and Nigel Whitmey, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. The officers are dismayed to learn the arson attack on the Osgoods' house has injured Michael and killed his son Robbie, and local bully Gary McCready is soon questioned. As the investigation into Katrin's murder continues, the body of an older man who died of natural causes is found buried near her grave, and when Banks suspects Jason McCready abducted missing Annika, he uncovers a shocking secret. Meanwhile, the detective gives a moving speech at his mother's funeral, and the ceremony brings him closer to his father

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A Belgian film star arrives in London and summons her friend Poirot to unravel the mysterious threats she has been receiving, which demand the return of a priceless diamond, the Western Star. Rosalind Bennett guest stars

Discreetly continuing his enquiries, he is intrigued to learn the local priest recently heard a very disturbing confession. Starring David Jason, Eoin McCarthy, Anthony Calf, Kenneth Cope and Isla Blair

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jamie looks after the pigeons while Jack is in hospital. Emily offers Ken support in his fight to get Daniel back. Deirdre tells Barlow they need to talk

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alf celebrates getting his licence renewed. Fred fears he has ruined his chances with Rita. Vera struggles with the Rovers when half the staff call in sick

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kathy and Eric argue about the incident in her flat. Linda presses Zoe to find out why Sophie has left Emmerdale. Jack tells Rachel he will give up everything for her

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jack apologises to Rachel about the previous night. Tara thanks Kim for saving her. Sophie tells Zoe she cannot bear it if she is ashamed of her

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A local man confesses to a girl's murder of 11 years previously, prompting Sgt Miller to reopen the case. A search fails to uncover the victim's body, but the investigation takes an intriguing twist when PC Younger unearths a male skeleton instead. Meanwhile, Peggy sets her sights on a wealthy widower. Rural drama, guest starring Graham Turner and Geoffrey Hutchings, with John Duttine, Steven Blakeley and Gwen Taylor

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin uses the threat of a roving mouse as an excuse to spend the night in Chrissy's bedroom. Comedy series, with Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy discovers she has an admirer, while Mr Roper thinks his wife is seeing someone else. Comedy series, with Richard O'Sullivan and Brian Murphy

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: George's brother discovers documents that prove Mildred married an Army deserter. Comedy, starring Yootha Joyce and Brian Murphy

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A woman's body is pulled from the river, reminding Frost that he investigated the death of her husband's first wife years ago. Unfortunately, it turns out that a key witness's statement went astray during the earlier case - which leads to Frost being suspended from duty. Discreetly continuing his enquiries, he is intrigued to learn the local priest recently heard a very disturbing confession. Starring David Jason, Eoin McCarthy, Anthony Calf, Kenneth Cope and Isla Blair

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A group of young offenders come to stay at Ashfordly Hall, but soon find themselves under suspicion after a spate of disturbances in the village. Meanwhile, Blaketon and Ventress go on the hunt for a spinster's missing dog, and a £50 reward gives Peggy an idea. Rural drama, starring Rupert Vansittart, William Simons, Derek Fowlds and Gwen Taylor

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Tony and Becky anxiously wait for Will's GCSE results. Alex tells Kim he feels guilty about their one-night stand. Lisa admits to Mandy she is jealous of Zak's feelings for Becky

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Lisa is still upset with Zak for flirting with Becky. Sophie tells Kim that she wants to move in with Zoe. Steve confronts Kim with some unwelcome home truths and she storms out

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alec is furious that Fiona has let him down. Curly is stunned when Angie Freeman looks him up. Liz receives a call from a frightened Steve. Vera can't cope anymore in the pub

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ken enjoys a weekend with Daniel. Fiona celebrates her 21st birthday. Alec agrees to help Vera out at the Rovers. Judy accuses Joyce of dipping into other people's catalogue money

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A horse whisperer disappears in Newton Magna, bringing Barnaby face to face with long-standing adversary Melvyn Stockard. The former convict's social climbing has paid dividends, sending him straight to the top of the village pecking order - and some believe his driving ambition makes him capable of foul play. Murder mystery, guest starring Larry Lamb (EastEnders, Gavin & Stacey), with John Nettles and Daniel Casey

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Pawnbroker Peter Lawson is shot dead in an armed robbery at his premises and Frost's investigations unearth a tragic family secret. He is convinced Lawson was killed by one of his children in revenge for years of systematic sexual abuse - but the truth turns out to be even more chilling. David Jason stars, with Diane Keen, Isla Blair, Matt Bardock, Anthony Bate, Mark Lambert and Edward Purver

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Midsomer University science fellow George Jeffers threatens to make public a problem with his state-of-the-art invention Kernel Logic, which he believes is potentially lethal, but American software boss Clinton Finn fears he will lose millions if the truth comes out. When a schoolteacher is killed in a hit-and-run, Barnaby suspects Jeffers was the intended target. Detective drama, guest starring David Haig and Nigel Whitmey, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. The officers are dismayed to learn the arson attack on the Osgoods' house has injured Michael and killed his son Robbie, and local bully Gary McCready is soon questioned. As the investigation into Katrin's murder continues, the body of an older man who died of natural causes is found buried near her grave, and when Banks suspects Jason McCready abducted missing Annika, he uncovers a shocking secret. Meanwhile, the detective gives a moving speech at his mother's funeral, and the ceremony brings him closer to his father

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A Belgian film star arrives in London and summons her friend Poirot to unravel the mysterious threats she has been receiving, which demand the return of a priceless diamond, the Western Star. Rosalind Bennett guest stars

programme.title: British Touring Car Championship Highlights
programme.desc: Steve Rider presents action from rounds 16, 17 and 18 of the 2020 Championship from Silverstone. Commentary comes from David Addison and Tim Harvey, with Louise Goodman reporting

programme.title: French Open Tennis: Roland-Garros Live
programme.desc: Coverage of the eighth day of the Grand Slam, staged at Roland Garros in Paris, featuring the opening fourth-round matches in the men's and women's draws. Great Britain's Johanna Konta overcame Croatian Donna Vekic 6-2, 6-4 at this stage last year, while Sloane Stephens eliminated 2016 champion Garbine Muguruza. In the men's competition, Roger Federer and Rafael Nadal both prevailed 6-2, 6-3, 6-3 against Leonardo Mayer and Juan Ignacio Londero respectively. John Inverdale presents, with analysis from Jim Courier, Marion Bartoli, Anne Keothavong, Mark Petchey, Sam Smith and Fabrice Santoro, commentary by Nick Mullins and Jonathan Overend, and reports from Celina Hinchcliffe

programme.title: In the Line of Fire (1993)
programme.desc: An ageing secret service agent, tormented by his failure to save the life of John F Kennedy in 1963, is drawn into a battle of wits with a sinister CIA-trained assassin out to kill the current president. As the day of reckoning draws closer, the agent's nerves are tested to the limit - and nothing seems to be going his way. Wolfgang Petersen's thriller, starring Clint Eastwood, John Malkovich, Rene Russo and Dylan McDermott

Drama, starring George Cole and Gary Webster

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Steed finds himself having to trust agent Melville, who has lost three partners and seems to be lying constantly, as he attempts to convict Lord Edgefield of blackmail. Patrick Macnee stars

programme.title: French Open Tennis: Roland-Garros Live
programme.desc: Coverage of the ninth day of the Grand Slam, staged at Roland Garros in Paris, featuring the concluding fourth-round matches in the men's and women's draws. Top seed Novak Djokovic prevailed in straight sets against Jan-Lennard Struff in day nine last year, before Dominic Thiem ended the dreams of home favourite Gael Monfils with a 6-4, 6-4, 6-2 triumph. In the women's competition, eventual champion Ashleigh Barty defeated Sofia Kenin 6-3, 3-6, 6-0, and 2018 title-winner Simona Halep overwhelmed Iga Swiatek for the loss of just one game. John Inverdale presents, with analysis from Jim Courier, Marion Bartoli, Anne Keothavong, Mark Petchey, Sam Smith and Fabrice Santoro, commentary by Nick Mullins and Jonathan Overend, and reports from Celina Hinchcliffe

programme.title: First Blood (1982)
programme.desc: Vietnam veteran John Rambo is wrongfully arrested in a backwoods town and tested to the limits of his endurance by a brutal deputy. Rambo escapes from his cell and heads out to the woods, and with the forces of the law in hot pursuit he finally has the chance to fight them on his own terms. Action thriller, starring Sylvester Stallone, Brian Dennehy, Richard Crenna and David Caruso

programme.title: French Open Tennis: Roland-Garros Live
programme.desc: John Inverdale presents live coverage of day 10, featuring the opening quarter-finals in the Grand Slam at Roland-Garros. Johanna Konta became the first British woman to reach the semi-finals of this tournament since Jo Durie in 1983 when she defeated Sloane Stephens 6-1, 6-4 at this stage last year, while Roger Federer prevailed in four sets against Swiss compatriot Stan Wawrinka. Match analysis comes from two-time former champion Jim Courier, former Wimbledon champion Marion Bartoli, British Fed Cup captain Anne Keothavong, Andy Murray's former coach Mark Petchey, Fabrice Santoro - who played in 70 Grand Slams - and former British number one Sam Smith. Commentary is from Nick Mullins and Jonathan Overend, with Celina Hinchcliffe reporting

programme.title: World Superbike Highlights
programme.desc: Action from the seventh round of the season south of Paris, as the riders competed for glory at the Circuit de Nevers Magny-Cours in France. Valuable points were up for grabs, but who snatched glory at the 4.411km track in the Pirelli French Round?

programme.title: Isle of Man TT
programme.desc: A look back at the most memorable superbike races in the last 10 years of the road-racing event, as the competitors raced over the mountain course

programme.title: Lethal Weapon
programme.desc: Riggs and Murtaugh respond to a noise complaint at a party at a heavyweight boxer Hollywood hills home. When gunshots go off, they are drawn into a more important case involving illegal gun-runners with advanced military technology. Riggs becomes personally invested in matters when he discovers one of their targets is a pregnant woman who reminds him of his late wife. Meanwhile, Trish worries about Murtaugh's health, as his partnership with Riggs tests his limits. Clayne Crawford and Damon Wayans star

programme.desc: Fizz writes a genius plan on a note that gets stuck to Magoo's forehead. When Weasel tries to get it back, Magoo thinks he's being pursued by spies.

programme.desc: Principal Skinner introduces a unique incentive to stop pupils being naughty - a ride on a submarine.
